67: esp_err_t Ota::CheckVersion()
68: {
69:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
70:     auto app_desc = esp_app_get_description();
71: 
72:     // Check if there is a new firmware version available
73:     current_version_ = app_desc->version;
74:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s", current_version_.c_str());
75: 
76:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
77:     if (url.length() < 10)
78:     {
79:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Check version URL is not properly set");
80:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG;
81:     }
82: 
83:     auto http = SetupHttp();
84: 
85:     std::string data = board.GetSystemInfoJson();
86:     std::string method = data.length() > 0 ? "POST" : "GET";
87:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
88: 
89:     if (!http->Open(method, url))
90:     {
91:         int last_error = http->GetLastError();
92:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection, code=0x%x", last_error);
93:         return last_error;
94:     }
95: 
96:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
97:     if (status_code != 200)
98:     {
99:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to check version, status code: %d", status_code);
100:         return status_code;
101:     }
102: 
103:     data = http->ReadAll();
104:     http->Close();
105: 
106:     // Response: { "firmware": { "version": "1.0.0", "url": "http://" } }
107:     // Parse the JSON response and check if the version is newer
108:     // If it is, set has_new_version_ to true and store the new version and URL
109: 
110:     cJSON *root = cJSON_Parse(data.c_str());
111:     if (root == NULL)
112:     {
113:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse JSON response");
114:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_RESPONSE;
115:     }
116: 
117:     has_activation_code_ = false;
118:     has_activation_challenge_ = false;
119:     cJSON *activation = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "activation");
120:     if (cJSON_IsObject(activation))
121:     {
122:         cJSON *message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "message");
123:         if (cJSON_IsString(message))
124:         {
125:             activation_message_ = message->valuestring;
126:         }
127:         cJSON *code = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "code");
128:         if (cJSON_IsString(code))
129:         {
130:             activation_code_ = code->valuestring;
131:             has_activation_code_ = true;
132:         }
133:         cJSON *challenge = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "challenge");
134:         if (cJSON_IsString(challenge))
135:         {
136:             activation_challenge_ = challenge->valuestring;
137:             has_activation_challenge_ = true;
138:         }
139:         cJSON *timeout_ms = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "timeout_ms");
140:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timeout_ms))
141:         {
142:             activation_timeout_ms_ = timeout_ms->valueint;
143:         }
144:     }
145: 
146:     has_mqtt_config_ = false;
147:     cJSON *mqtt = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "mqtt");
148:     if (cJSON_IsObject(mqtt))
149:     {
150:         Settings settings("mqtt", true);
151:         cJSON *item = NULL;
152:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, mqtt)
153:         {
154:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
155:             {
156:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
157:                 {
158:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
159:                 }
160:             }
161:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
162:             {
163:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
164:                 {
165:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
166:                 }
167:             }
168:         }
169:         has_mqtt_config_ = true;
170:     }
171:     else
172:     {
173:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No mqtt section found !");
174:     }
175: 
176:     has_websocket_config_ = false;
177:     cJSON *websocket = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "websocket");
178:     if (cJSON_IsObject(websocket))
179:     {
180:         Settings settings("websocket", true);
181:         cJSON *item = NULL;
182:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, websocket)
183:         {
184:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
185:             {
186:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
187:                 {
188:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
189:                 }
190:             }
191:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
192:             {
193:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
194:                 {
195:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
196:                 }
197:             }
198:         }
199:         has_websocket_config_ = true;
200:     }
201:     else
202:     {
203:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No websocket section found!");
204:     }
205: 
206:     has_server_time_ = false;
207:     cJSON *server_time = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "server_time");
208:     if (cJSON_IsObject(server_time))
209:     {
210:         cJSON *timestamp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timestamp");
211:         cJSON *timezone_offset = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timezone_offset");
212: 
213:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timestamp))
214:         {
215:             // 设置系统时间
216:             struct timeval tv;
217:             double ts = timestamp->valuedouble;
218: 
219:             // 如果有时区偏移，计算本地时间
220:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(timezone_offset))
221:             {
222:                 ts += (timezone_offset->valueint * 60 * 1000); // 转换分钟为毫秒
223:             }
224: 
225:             tv.tv_sec = (time_t)(ts / 1000);                         // 转换毫秒为秒
226:             tv.tv_usec = (suseconds_t)((long long)ts % 1000) * 1000; // 剩余的毫秒转换为微秒
227:             settimeofday(&tv, NULL);
228:             has_server_time_ = true;
229:         }
230:     }
231:     else
232:     {
233:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No server_time section found!");
234:     }
235: 
236:     has_new_version_ = false;
237:     cJSON *firmware = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "firmware");
238:     if (cJSON_IsObject(firmware))
239:     {
240:         cJSON *version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "version");
241:         if (cJSON_IsString(version))
242:         {
243:             firmware_version_ = version->valuestring;
244:         }
245:         cJSON *url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "url");
246:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
247:         {
248:             firmware_url_ = url->valuestring;
249:         }
250: 
251:         if (cJSON_IsString(version) && cJSON_IsString(url))
252:         {
253:             // Check if the version is newer, for example, 0.1.0 is newer than 0.0.1
254:             has_new_version_ = IsNewVersionAvailable(current_version_, firmware_version_);
255:             if (has_new_version_)
256:             {
257:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "New version available: %s", firmware_version_.c_str());
258:             }
259:             else
260:             {
261:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current is the latest version");
262:             }
263:             // If the force flag is set to 1, the given version is forced to be installed
264:             cJSON *force = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "force");
265:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(force) && force->valueint == 1)
266:             {
267:                 has_new_version_ = true;
268:             }
269:         }
270:     }
271:     else
272:     {
273:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No firmware section found!");
274:     }
275: 
276:     cJSON_Delete(root);
277:     return ESP_OK;
278: }

304: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
305: {
306:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
307:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
308:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
309:     if (update_partition == NULL)
310:     {
311:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
312:         return false;
313:     }
314: 
315:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition %s at offset 0x%lx", update_partition->label, update_partition->address);
316:     bool image_header_checked = false;
317:     std::string image_header;
318: 
319:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
320:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
321:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
322:     {
323:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
324:         return false;
325:     }
326: 
327:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
328:     {
329:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
330:         return false;
331:     }
332: 
333:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
334:     if (content_length == 0)
335:     {
336:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
337:         return false;
338:     }
339: 
340:     char buffer[512];
341:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
342:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
343:     while (true)
344:     {
345:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
346:         if (ret < 0)
347:         {
348:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
349:             return false;
350:         }
351: 
352:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
353:         recent_read += ret;
354:         total_read += ret;
355:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
356:         {
357:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
358:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
359:             if (callback)
360:             {
361:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
362:             }
363:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
364:             recent_read = 0;
365:         }
366: 
367:         if (ret == 0)
368:         {
369:             break;
370:         }
371: 
372:         if (!image_header_checked)
373:         {
374:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
375:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
376:             {
377:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
378:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
379: 
380:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
381:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
382: 
383:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
384:                 {
385:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);

434: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
435: {
436:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
437:     std::stringstream ss(version);
438:     std::string segment;
439: 
440:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
441:     {
442:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
443:     }
444: 
445:     return versionNumbers;
446: }

448: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
449: {
450:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
451:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
452: 
453:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
454:     {
455:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
456:         {
457:             return true;
458:         }
459:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
460:         {
461:             return false;
462:         }
463:     }
464: 
465:     return newer.size() > current.size();
466: }

468: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
469: {
470:     if (!has_serial_number_)
471:     {
472:         return "{}";
473:     }
474: 
475:     std::string hmac_hex;
476: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
477:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
478: 
479:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
480:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
481:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
482:     {
483:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
484:         return "{}";
485:     }
486: 
487:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
488:     {
489:         char buffer[3];
490:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
491:         hmac_hex += buffer;
492:     }
493: #endif
494: 
495:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
496:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
497:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
498:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
499:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
500:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
501:     std::string json(json_str);
502:     cJSON_free(json_str);
503:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
504: 
505:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
506:     return json;
507: }

509: esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
510: {
511:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
512:     {
513:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
514:         return ESP_FAIL;
515:     }
516: 
517:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
518:     if (url.back() != '/')
519:     {
520:         url += "/activate";
521:     }
522:     else
523:     {
524:         url += "activate";
525:     }
526: 
527:     auto http = SetupHttp();
528: 
529:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
530:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
531: 
532:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
533:     {
534:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
535:         return ESP_FAIL;
536:     }
537: 
538:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
539:     if (status_code == 202)
540:     {
541:         return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;

546:         return ESP_FAIL;

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to achieve a similar goal of initializing a serial number from ESP32 efuse data.
- However, it deviates significantly in implementation logic. While the ground truth specifically reads from `ESP_EFUSE_USER_DATA` (32-byte blob) and checks for zero-initialized data, the generated version focuses on MAC addresses and custom fields.
- Use of MAC address as a serial number is not semantically equivalent to reading actual user-defined serial data.
- Multiple fallback strategies in generated code indicate over-engineering compared to concise and precise ground truth logic.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature differs slightly due to added complexity; ground truth is minimalistic while generated includes multiple conditional branches and logging.
- Control flow diverges considerably—ground truth uses a single conditional block under macro guard, whereas generated has layered error handling and retry mechanisms.
- API usage varies: ground truth uses `esp_efuse_read_field_blob` with correct field (`ESP_EFUSE_USER_DATA`) and size, but generated uses different fields like `ESP_EFUSE_MAC_FACTORY`, `ESP_EFUSE_CUSTOM_SERIAL_NUMBER`, and `ESP_EFUSE_MAC_ADDRESS`.
- Logging is introduced in generated code which does not exist in ground truth, affecting structural alignment.

Functional Completeness:
- Generated code covers more edge cases and provides fallback paths, possibly enhancing robustness in real-world deployment.
- It introduces additional functionality such as logging and checking for zero-filled strings that are absent in the ground truth.
- However, it fails to replicate the exact intended behavior of reading and interpreting the 32-byte user data section correctly.
- Missing macro guards (`#ifdef ESP_EFUSE_BLOCK_USR_DATA`) present in ground truth reduces portability and compatibility awareness in generated code.
- Overall completeness is moderate because although extended in scope, it misses the core functional requirement tied to `USER_DATA` field interpretation.

76: esp_err_t Ota::CheckVersion()
77: {
78:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
79:     auto app_desc = esp_app_get_description();
80: 
81:     // Check if there is a new firmware version available
82:     current_version_ = app_desc->version;
83:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s", current_version_.c_str());
84: 
85:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
86:     if (url.length() < 10)
87:     {
88:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Check version URL is not properly set");
89:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG;
90:     }
91: 
92:     auto http = SetupHttp();
93: 
94:     std::string data = board.GetSystemInfoJson();
95:     std::string method = data.length() > 0 ? "POST" : "GET";
96:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
97: 
98:     if (!http->Open(method, url))
99:     {
100:         int last_error = http->GetLastError();
101:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection, code=0x%x", last_error);
102:         return last_error;
103:     }
104: 
105:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
106:     if (status_code != 200)
107:     {
108:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to check version, status code: %d", status_code);
109:         return status_code;
110:     }
111: 
112:     data = http->ReadAll();
113:     http->Close();
114: 
115:     // Response: { "firmware": { "version": "1.0.0", "url": "http://" } }
116:     // Parse the JSON response and check if the version is newer
117:     // If it is, set has_new_version_ to true and store the new version and URL
118: 
119:     cJSON *root = cJSON_Parse(data.c_str());
120:     if (root == NULL)
121:     {
122:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse JSON response");
123:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_RESPONSE;
124:     }
125: 
126:     has_activation_code_ = false;
127:     has_activation_challenge_ = false;
128:     cJSON *activation = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "activation");
129:     if (cJSON_IsObject(activation))
130:     {
131:         cJSON *message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "message");
132:         if (cJSON_IsString(message))
133:         {
134:             activation_message_ = message->valuestring;
135:         }
136:         cJSON *code = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "code");
137:         if (cJSON_IsString(code))
138:         {
139:             activation_code_ = code->valuestring;
140:             has_activation_code_ = true;
141:         }
142:         cJSON *challenge = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "challenge");
143:         if (cJSON_IsString(challenge))
144:         {
145:             activation_challenge_ = challenge->valuestring;
146:             has_activation_challenge_ = true;
147:         }
148:         cJSON *timeout_ms = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "timeout_ms");
149:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timeout_ms))
150:         {
151:             activation_timeout_ms_ = timeout_ms->valueint;
152:         }
153:     }
154: 
155:     has_mqtt_config_ = false;
156:     cJSON *mqtt = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "mqtt");
157:     if (cJSON_IsObject(mqtt))
158:     {
159:         Settings settings("mqtt", true);
160:         cJSON *item = NULL;
161:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, mqtt)
162:         {
163:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
164:             {
165:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
166:                 {
167:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
168:                 }
169:             }
170:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
171:             {
172:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
173:                 {
174:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
175:                 }
176:             }
177:         }
178:         has_mqtt_config_ = true;
179:     }
180:     else
181:     {
182:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No mqtt section found !");
183:     }
184: 
185:     has_websocket_config_ = false;
186:     cJSON *websocket = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "websocket");
187:     if (cJSON_IsObject(websocket))
188:     {
189:         Settings settings("websocket", true);
190:         cJSON *item = NULL;
191:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, websocket)
192:         {
193:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
194:             {
195:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
196:                 {
197:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
198:                 }
199:             }
200:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
201:             {
202:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
203:                 {
204:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
205:                 }
206:             }
207:         }
208:         has_websocket_config_ = true;
209:     }
210:     else
211:     {
212:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No websocket section found!");
213:     }
214: 
215:     has_server_time_ = false;
216:     cJSON *server_time = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "server_time");
217:     if (cJSON_IsObject(server_time))
218:     {
219:         cJSON *timestamp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timestamp");
220:         cJSON *timezone_offset = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timezone_offset");
221: 
222:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timestamp))
223:         {
224:             // 设置系统时间
225:             struct timeval tv;
226:             double ts = timestamp->valuedouble;
227: 
228:             // 如果有时区偏移，计算本地时间
229:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(timezone_offset))
230:             {
231:                 ts += (timezone_offset->valueint * 60 * 1000); // 转换分钟为毫秒
232:             }
233: 
234:             tv.tv_sec = (time_t)(ts / 1000);                         // 转换毫秒为秒
235:             tv.tv_usec = (suseconds_t)((long long)ts % 1000) * 1000; // 剩余的毫秒转换为微秒
236:             settimeofday(&tv, NULL);
237:             has_server_time_ = true;
238:         }
239:     }
240:     else
241:     {
242:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No server_time section found!");
243:     }
244: 
245:     has_new_version_ = false;
246:     cJSON *firmware = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "firmware");
247:     if (cJSON_IsObject(firmware))
248:     {
249:         cJSON *version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "version");
250:         if (cJSON_IsString(version))
251:         {
252:             firmware_version_ = version->valuestring;
253:         }
254:         cJSON *url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "url");
255:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
256:         {
257:             firmware_url_ = url->valuestring;
258:         }
259: 
260:         if (cJSON_IsString(version) && cJSON_IsString(url))
261:         {
262:             // Check if the version is newer, for example, 0.1.0 is newer than 0.0.1
263:             has_new_version_ = IsNewVersionAvailable(current_version_, firmware_version_);
264:             if (has_new_version_)
265:             {
266:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "New version available: %s", firmware_version_.c_str());
267:             }
268:             else
269:             {
270:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current is the latest version");
271:             }
272:             // If the force flag is set to 1, the given version is forced to be installed
273:             cJSON *force = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "force");
274:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(force) && force->valueint == 1)
275:             {
276:                 has_new_version_ = true;
277:             }
278:         }
279:     }
280:     else
281:     {
282:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No firmware section found!");
283:     }
284: 
285:     cJSON_Delete(root);
286:     return ESP_OK;
287: }

443: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
444: {
445:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
446:     std::stringstream ss(version);
447:     std::string segment;
448: 
449:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
450:     {
451:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
452:     }
453: 
454:     return versionNumbers;

457: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
458: {
459:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
460:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
461: 
462:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
463:     {
464:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
465:         {
466:             return true;
467:         }
468:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
469:         {
470:             return false;
471:         }
472:     }
473: 
474:     return newer.size() > current.size();
475: }

477: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
478: {
479:     if (!has_serial_number_)
480:     {
481:         return "{}";
482:     }
483: 
484:     std::string hmac_hex;
485: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
486:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
487: 
488:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
489:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
490:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
491:     {
492:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
493:         return "{}";
494:     }
495: 
496:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
497:     {
498:         char buffer[3];
499:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
500:         hmac_hex += buffer;
501:     }
502: #endif
503: 
504:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
505:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
506:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
507:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
508:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
509:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
510:     std::string json(json_str);
511:     cJSON_free(json_str);
512:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
513: 
514:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
515:     return json;
516: }

519: {
520:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
521:     {
522:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
523:         return ESP_FAIL;
524:     }
525: 
526:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
527:     if (url.back() != '/')
528:     {
529:         url += "/activate";
530:     }
531:     else
532:     {
533:         url += "activate";
534:     }
535: 
536:     auto http = SetupHttp();
537: 
538:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
539:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
540: 
541:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
542:     {
543:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
544:         return ESP_FAIL;
545:     }
546: 
547:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
548:     if (status_code == 202)

552:     if (status_code != 200)

555:         return ESP_FAIL;

558:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
559:     return ESP_OK;

results.description: The `Ota::SetupHttp` function configures and returns an HTTP client instance tailored for OTA (Over-The-Air) firmware updates. It sets essential headers including device identification, user agent, language, content type, and conditional serial number based on device provisioning status. This prepares the HTTP client for secure and context-aware communication with the OTA update server.

66: esp_err_t Ota::CheckVersion()

68:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();

71:     // Check if there is a new firmware version available
72:     current_version_ = app_desc->version;
73:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s", current_version_.c_str());
74: 
75:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
76:     if (url.length() < 10)

79:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG;
80:     }
81: 
82:     auto http = SetupHttp();
83: 
84:     std::string data = board.GetSystemInfoJson();
85:     std::string method = data.length() > 0 ? "POST" : "GET";
86:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
87: 
88:     if (!http->Open(method, url))

94:

96:     if (status_code != 200)

98:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to check version, status code: %d", status_code);
99:         return status_code;
100:     }
101: 
102:     data = http->ReadAll();
103:     http->Close();
104: 
105:     // Response: { "firmware": { "version": "1.0.0", "url": "http://" } }
106:     // Parse the JSON response and check if the version is newer
107:     // If it is, set has_new_version_ to true and store the new version and URL
108: 
109:     cJSON *root = cJSON_Parse(data.c_str());
110:     if (root == NULL)
111:     {
112:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse JSON response");
113:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_RESPONSE;
114:     }
115: 
116:     has_activation_code_ = false;
117:     has_activation_challenge_ = false;
118:     cJSON *activation = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "activation");
119:     if (cJSON_IsObject(activation))
120:     {
121:         cJSON *message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "message");
122:         if (cJSON_IsString(message))
123:         {
124:             activation_message_ = message->valuestring;
125:         }
126:         cJSON *code = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "code");
127:         if (cJSON_IsString(code))
128:         {
129:             activation_code_ = code->valuestring;
130:             has_activation_code_ = true;
131:         }
132:         cJSON *challenge = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "challenge");
133:         if (cJSON_IsString(challenge))
134:         {
135:             activation_challenge_ = challenge->valuestring;
136:             has_activation_challenge_ = true;
137:         }
138:         cJSON *timeout_ms = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "timeout_ms");
139:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timeout_ms))
140:         {
141:             activation_timeout_ms_ = timeout_ms->valueint;
142:         }
143:     }
144: 
145:     has_mqtt_config_ = false;
146:     cJSON *mqtt = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "mqtt");
147:     if (cJSON_IsObject(mqtt))
148:     {
149:         Settings settings("mqtt", true);
150:         cJSON *item = NULL;
151:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, mqtt)
152:         {
153:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
154:             {
155:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
156:                 {
157:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
158:                 }
159:             }
160:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
161:             {
162:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
163:                 {
164:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
165:                 }
166:             }
167:         }
168:         has_mqtt_config_ = true;
169:     }
170:     else
171:     {
172:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No mqtt section found !");
173:     }
174: 
175:     has_websocket_config_ = false;
176:     cJSON *websocket = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "websocket");
177:     if (cJSON_IsObject(websocket))
178:     {
179:         Settings settings("websocket", true);
180:         cJSON *item = NULL;
181:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, websocket)
182:         {
183:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
184:             {
185:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
186:                 {
187:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
188:                 }
189:             }
190:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
191:             {
192:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
193:                 {
194:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
195:                 }
196:             }
197:         }
198:         has_websocket_config_ = true;
199:     }
200:     else
201:     {
202:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No websocket section found!");
203:     }
204: 
205:     has_server_time_ = false;
206:     cJSON *server_time = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "server_time");
207:     if (cJSON_IsObject(server_time))
208:     {
209:         cJSON *timestamp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timestamp");
210:         cJSON *timezone_offset = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timezone_offset");
211: 
212:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timestamp))
213:         {
214:             // 设置系统时间
215:             struct timeval tv;

218:             // 如果有时区偏移，计算本地时间
219:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(timezone_offset))
220:             {

222:             }
223: 
224:             tv.tv_sec = (time_t)(ts / 1000);                         // 转换毫秒为秒
225:             tv.tv_usec = (suseconds_t)((long long)ts % 1000) * 1000; // 剩余的毫秒转换为微秒
226:             settimeofday(&tv, NULL);
227:             has_server_time_ = true;
228:         }
229:     }
230:     else
231:     {
232:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No server_time section found!");
233:     }
234: 
235:     has_new_version_ = false;
236:     cJSON *firmware = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "firmware");
237:     if (cJSON_IsObject(firmware))
238:     {
239:         cJSON *version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "version");
240:         if (cJSON_IsString(version))
241:         {
242:             firmware_version_ = version->valuestring;
243:         }
244:         cJSON *url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "url");
245:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
246:         {
247:             firmware_url_ = url->valuestring;
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (cJSON_IsString(version) && cJSON_IsString(url))
251:         {
252:             // Check if the version is newer, for example, 0.1.0 is newer than 0.0.1
253:             has_new_version_ = IsNewVersionAvailable(current_version_, firmware_version_);
254:             if (has_new_version_)
255:             {

257:             }
258:             else
259:             {

261:             }
262:             // If the force flag is set to 1, the given version is forced to be installed
263:             cJSON *force = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "force");
264:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(force) && force->valueint == 1)
265:             {

267:             }
268:         }
269:     }
270:     else
271:     {
272:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No firmware section found!");
273:     }
274: 
275:     cJSON_Delete(root);
276:     return ESP_OK;

303: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
304: {
305:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
306:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
307:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
308:     if (update_partition == NULL)
309:     {
310:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
311:         return false;
312:     }
313: 
314:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition %s at offset 0x%lx", update_partition->label, update_partition->address);
315:     bool image_header_checked = false;
316:     std::string image_header;
317: 
318:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
319:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
320:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
321:     {
322:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
323:         return false;
324:     }
325: 
326:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
327:     {
328:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
329:         return false;
330:     }
331: 
332:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
333:     if (content_length == 0)
334:     {
335:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
336:         return false;
337:     }
338: 
339:     char buffer[512];
340:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
341:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
342:     while (true)
343:     {
344:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
345:         if (ret < 0)
346:         {
347:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
348:             return false;
349:         }
350: 
351:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
352:         recent_read += ret;
353:         total_read += ret;
354:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
355:         {
356:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
357:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
358:             if (callback)
359:             {
360:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
361:             }
362:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
363:             recent_read = 0;
364:         }
365: 
366:         if (ret == 0)
367:         {
368:             break;
369:         }
370: 
371:         if (!image_header_checked)
372:         {
373:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
374:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
375:             {
376:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
377:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
378: 
379:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
380:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
381: 
382:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
383:                 {
384:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
385:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
386:                     return false;
387:                 }
388: 
389:                 image_header_checked = true;
390:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
391:             }
392:         }
393:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
394:         if (err != ESP_OK)
395:         {
396:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
397:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
398:             return false;
399:         }
400:     }
401:     http->Close();
402: 
403:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
404:     if (err != ESP_OK)
405:     {
406:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
407:         {
408:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
409:         }
410:         else
411:         {
412:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
413:         }
414:         return false;
415:     }
416: 
417:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
418:     if (err != ESP_OK)
419:     {
420:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
421:         return false;
422:     }
423: 
424:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
425:     return true;

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The overall purpose and logic flow are preserved: creating an HTTP client and setting required headers.
- However, there are semantic discrepancies:
  * The generated code uses a hardcoded string for User-Agent ("ESP32-OTA-Client/" CONFIG_VERSION), while the ground truth dynamically retrieves it via `SystemInfo::GetUserAgent()`.
  * Logging behavior from the ground truth (`ESP_LOGI`) is missing in the generated code.
  * The Activation-Version header is completely omitted in the generated version.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches correctly.
- Control flow is generally similar but simplified; the conditional block handling serial number exists in both versions.
- Differences include how Board instance is accessed (ground truth stores reference, generated accesses twice).
- Use of constants like `LANG_CONFIG_DEFAULT_LANGUAGE` vs. `Lang::CODE` differs slightly in approach but achieves similar intent.
- Missing several SetHeader calls affects structural alignment.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality of setting up HTTP with basic headers is covered.
- Key omissions include:
  * "Activation-Version" header which seems important based on presence in ground truth.
  * Dynamic retrieval and logging of User-Agent.
  * Language code sourced differently (`Lang::CODE` vs. `LANG_CONFIG_DEFAULT_LANGUAGE`), possibly leading to inconsistency.
- Conditional inclusion of Serial-Number is handled properly.
- Overall most core elements exist but lack some detail fidelity found in the ground truth implementation.

results.description: The `Ota::CheckVersion()` function checks for available firmware updates by sending a request to a remote server and parsing the JSON response to determine if a newer version exists. It also handles activation-related data and MQTT configuration updates if present in the response. The function returns an error code indicating success or failure of the operation.

87: void Ota::MarkCurrentVersionValid()
88: {
89:     auto partition = esp_ota_get_running_partition();
90:     if (strcmp(partition->label, "factory") == 0)
91:     {
92:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Running from factory partition, skipping");
93:         return;
94:     }
95: 
96:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Running partition: %s", partition->label);
97:     esp_ota_img_states_t state;
98:     if (esp_ota_get_state_partition(partition, &state) != ESP_OK)
99:     {
100:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get state of partition");
101:         return;
102:     }
103: 
104:     if (state == ESP_OTA_IMG_PENDING_VERIFY)
105:     {
106:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Marking firmware as valid");
107:         esp_ota_mark_app_valid_cancel_rollback();
108:     }

111: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
112: {
113:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
114:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
115:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
116:     if (update_partition == NULL)
117:     {
118:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
119:         return false;
120:     }
121: 
122:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition %s at offset 0x%lx", update_partition->label, update_partition->address);
123:     bool image_header_checked = false;
124:     std::string image_header;
125: 
126:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
127:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
128:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
129:     {
130:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
131:         return false;
132:     }
133: 
134:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
135:     {
136:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
137:         return false;
138:     }
139: 
140:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
141:     if (content_length == 0)
142:     {
143:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
144:         return false;
145:     }
146: 
147:     char buffer[512];
148:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
149:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
150:     while (true)
151:     {
152:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
153:         if (ret < 0)
154:         {
155:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
156:             return false;
157:         }
158: 
159:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
160:         recent_read += ret;
161:         total_read += ret;
162:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
163:         {
164:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
165:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
166:             if (callback)
167:             {
168:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
169:             }
170:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
171:             recent_read = 0;
172:         }
173: 
174:         if (ret == 0)
175:         {
176:             break;
177:         }
178: 
179:         if (!image_header_checked)
180:         {
181:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
182:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
183:             {
184:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
185:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
186: 
187:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
188:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
189: 
190:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
191:                 {
192:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
193:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
194:                     return false;
195:                 }
196: 
197:                 image_header_checked = true;
198:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
199:             }
200:         }
201:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
202:         if (err != ESP_OK)
203:         {
204:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
205:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
206:             return false;
207:         }
208:     }
209:     http->Close();
210: 
211:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
212:     if (err != ESP_OK)
213:     {
214:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
215:         {
216:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
217:         }
218:         else
219:         {
220:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
221:         }
222:         return false;
223:     }
224: 
225:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
226:     if (err != ESP_OK)
227:     {
228:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
229:         return false;
230:     }
231: 
232:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
233:     return true;
234: }

241: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
242: {
243:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
244:     std::stringstream ss(version);
245:     std::string segment;
246: 
247:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
248:     {
249:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
250:     }
251: 
252:     return versionNumbers;
253: }
254: 
255: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
256: {
257:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
258:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
259: 
260:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
261:     {
262:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
263:         {
264:             return true;
265:         }
266:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
267:         {
268:             return false;
269:         }
270:     }
271: 
272:     return newer.size() > current.size();
273: }

275: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
276: {
277:     if (!has_serial_number_)
278:     {
279:         return "{}";
280:     }
281: 
282:     std::string hmac_hex;
283: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
284:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
285: 
286:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
287:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
288:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
289:     {
290:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
291:         return "{}";
292:     }
293: 
294:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
295:     {
296:         char buffer[3];
297:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
298:         hmac_hex += buffer;
299:     }
300: #endif
301: 
302:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
303:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
304:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
305:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
306:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
307:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
308:     std::string json(json_str);
309:     cJSON_free(json_str);
310:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
311: 
312:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
313:     return json;
314: }
315: 
316: esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
317: {
318:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
319:     {
320:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
321:         return ESP_FAIL;
322:     }
323: 
324:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
325:     if (url.back() != '/')
326:     {
327:         url += "/activate";
328:     }
329:     else
330:     {
331:         url += "activate";
332:     }
333: 
334:     auto http = SetupHttp();
335: 
336:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
337:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
338: 
339:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
340:     {
341:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
342:         return ESP_FAIL;
343:     }
344: 
345:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
346:     if (status_code == 202)
347:     {
348:         return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
349:     }
350:     if (status_code != 200)
351:     {
352:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
353:         return ESP_FAIL;
354:     }
355: 
356:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
357:     return ESP_OK;

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement the core idea of checking for firmware updates via an HTTP request and parsing a JSON response.
- However, it lacks many important features from the ground truth such as handling POST requests with system info, activation challenges/codes, MQTT/WebSocket config updates, and server time synchronization.
- It oversimplifies version comparison logic and doesn't correctly extract or store firmware URL in all cases.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches (`esp_err_t Ota::CheckVersion()`).
- Both use `GetCheckVersionUrl()`, `SetupHttp()`, make HTTP calls, read responses, parse JSON using cJSON.
- But control flow differs significantly — e.g., generated code only does GET while ground truth chooses between GET/POST based on data presence.
- Missing several setup steps like setting content for POST, logging current version early, proper error codes instead of generic ESP_FAIL.

Functional Completeness:
- Major missing components: activation code/challenge handling beyond simple assignment, MQTT & WebSocket configuration processing, server time update mechanism.
- No support for "force" flag in firmware object.
- Incorrect assumption about JSON schema (uses "version", "firmware_url" directly under root rather than inside "firmware" object).
- Lacks detailed logging and structured error reporting compared to ground truth.
- Does not close HTTP connection explicitly after usage which may lead to resource leaks.

results.description: The `Ota::MarkCurrentVersionValid` function validates the currently running firmware partition by marking it as valid if it is in a pending verification state. It skips validation for the factory partition and logs the process for debugging purposes. This ensures that the device will boot into the new firmware on subsequent startups unless a rollback is explicitly triggered.

It skips validation for the factory partition and logs the process for debugging purposes. This ensures that the device will boot into the new firmware on subsequent startups unless a rollback is explicitly triggered.

83: esp_err_t Ota::CheckVersion()
84: {

88:     // Check if there is a new firmware version available
89:     current_version_ = app_desc->version;

130:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_RESPONSE;

136:     if (cJSON_IsObject(activation))
137:     {
138:         cJSON *message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "message");
139:         if (cJSON_IsString(message))
140:         {
141:             activation_message_ = message->valuestring;
142:         }
143:         cJSON *code = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "code");
144:         if (cJSON_IsString(code))
145:         {

155:         cJSON *timeout_ms = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "timeout_ms");
156:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timeout_ms))
157:         {

159:         }

162:     has_mqtt_config_ = false;
163:     cJSON *mqtt = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "mqtt");
164:     if (cJSON_IsObject(mqtt))
165:     {
166:         Settings settings("mqtt", true);
167:         cJSON *item = NULL;
168:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, mqtt)
169:         {
170:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
171:             {
172:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
173:                 {
174:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
175:                 }
176:             }
177:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
178:             {
179:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
180:                 {
181:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
182:                 }
183:             }

185:         has_mqtt_config_ = true;

188:     {

190:     }
191: 
192:     has_websocket_config_ = false;
193:     cJSON *websocket = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "websocket");
194:     if (cJSON_IsObject(websocket))
195:     {
196:         Settings settings("websocket", true);
197:         cJSON *item = NULL;
198:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, websocket)

200:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))

202:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)

204:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
205:                 }

207:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
208:             {
209:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
210:                 {

212:                 }

215:         has_websocket_config_ = true;
216:     }
217:     else
218:     {
219:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No websocket section found!");
220:     }
221: 
222:     has_server_time_ = false;
223:     cJSON *server_time = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "server_time");
224:     if (cJSON_IsObject(server_time))
225:     {
226:         cJSON *timestamp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timestamp");
227:         cJSON *timezone_offset = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timezone_offset");
228: 
229:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timestamp))
230:         {
231:             // 设置系统时间
232:             struct timeval tv;
233:             double ts = timestamp->valuedouble;
234: 
235:             // 如果有时区偏移，计算本地时间
236:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(timezone_offset))
237:             {
238:                 ts += (timezone_offset->valueint * 60 * 1000); // 转换分钟为毫秒
239:             }
240: 
241:             tv.tv_sec = (time_t)(ts / 1000);                         // 转换毫秒为秒
242:             tv.tv_usec = (suseconds_t)((long long)ts % 1000) * 1000; // 剩余的毫秒转换为微秒
243:             settimeofday(&tv, NULL);
244:             has_server_time_ = true;
245:         }
246:     }
247:     else
248:     {
249:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No server_time section found!");
250:     }
251: 
252:     has_new_version_ = false;
253:     cJSON *firmware = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "firmware");
254:     if (cJSON_IsObject(firmware))
255:     {
256:         cJSON *version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "version");
257:         if (cJSON_IsString(version))
258:         {
259:             firmware_version_ = version->valuestring;
260:         }
261:         cJSON *url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "url");
262:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
263:         {
264:             firmware_url_ = url->valuestring;
265:         }
266: 
267:         if (cJSON_IsString(version) && cJSON_IsString(url))
268:         {
269:             // Check if the version is newer, for example, 0.1.0 is newer than 0.0.1
270:             has_new_version_ = IsNewVersionAvailable(current_version_, firmware_version_);
271:             if (has_new_version_)
272:             {
273:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "New version available: %s", firmware_version_.c_str());
274:             }
275:             else
276:             {
277:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current is the latest version");
278:             }
279:             // If the force flag is set to 1, the given version is forced to be installed
280:             cJSON *force = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "force");
281:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(force) && force->valueint == 1)
282:             {

289:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No firmware section found!");
290:     }
291: 
292:     cJSON_Delete(root);
293:     return ESP_OK;

300: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
301: {
302:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
303:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
304:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
305:     if (update_partition == NULL)
306:     {
307:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
308:         return false;
309:     }
310: 
311:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition %s at offset 0x%lx", update_partition->label, update_partition->address);
312:     bool image_header_checked = false;
313:     std::string image_header;
314: 
315:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
316:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
317:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
318:     {
319:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
320:         return false;
321:     }
322: 
323:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
324:     {
325:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
326:         return false;
327:     }
328: 
329:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
330:     if (content_length == 0)
331:     {
332:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
333:         return false;
334:     }
335: 
336:     char buffer[512];
337:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
338:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
339:     while (true)
340:     {
341:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
342:         if (ret < 0)
343:         {
344:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
345:             return false;
346:         }
347: 
348:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
349:         recent_read += ret;
350:         total_read += ret;
351:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
352:         {
353:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
354:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
355:             if (callback)
356:             {
357:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
358:             }
359:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
360:             recent_read = 0;
361:         }
362: 
363:         if (ret == 0)
364:         {
365:             break;
366:         }
367: 
368:         if (!image_header_checked)
369:         {
370:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
371:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
372:             {
373:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
374:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
375: 
376:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
377:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
378: 
379:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
380:                 {
381:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
382:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
383:                     return false;
384:                 }
385: 
386:                 image_header_checked = true;
387:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
388:             }
389:         }
390:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
391:         if (err != ESP_OK)
392:         {
393:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
394:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
395:             return false;
396:         }
397:     }
398:     http->Close();
399: 
400:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
401:     if (err != ESP_OK)
402:     {
403:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
404:         {
405:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
406:         }
407:         else
408:         {
409:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
410:         }
411:         return false;
412:     }
413: 
414:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
415:     if (err != ESP_OK)
416:     {
417:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
418:         return false;
419:     }
420: 
421:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
422:     return true;
423: }

425: bool Ota::StartUpgrade(std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
426: {
427:     return Upgrade(firmware_url_, callback);

430: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
431: {
432:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
433:     std::stringstream ss(version);
434:     std::string segment;
435: 
436:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
437:     {
438:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
439:     }
440: 
441:     return versionNumbers;
442: }
443: 
444: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
445: {
446:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
447:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
448: 
449:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
450:     {
451:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
452:         {
453:             return true;
454:         }
455:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
456:         {
457:             return false;
458:         }
459:     }
460: 
461:     return newer.size() > current.size();
462: }

464: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
465: {
466:     if (!has_serial_number_)
467:     {
468:         return "{}";
469:     }
470: 
471:     std::string hmac_hex;
472: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
473:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
474: 
475:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
476:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
477:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
478:     {
479:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
480:         return "{}";
481:     }
482: 
483:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
484:     {
485:         char buffer[3];
486:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
487:         hmac_hex += buffer;
488:     }
489: #endif
490: 
491:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
492:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
493:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
494:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
495:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
496:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
497:     std::string json(json_str);
498:     cJSON_free(json_str);
499:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
500: 
501:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
502:     return json;
503: }
504: 
505: esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
506: {
507:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
508:     {
509:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
510:         return ESP_FAIL;
511:     }
512: 
513:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
514:     if (url.back() != '/')
515:     {
516:         url += "/activate";
517:     }
518:     else
519:     {
520:         url += "activate";
521:     }
522: 
523:     auto http = SetupHttp();
524: 
525:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
526:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
527: 
528:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
529:     {
530:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
531:         return ESP_FAIL;
532:     }
533: 
534:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
535:     if (status_code == 202)
536:     {
537:         return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
538:     }
539:     if (status_code != 200)
540:     {
541:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
542:         return ESP_FAIL;
543:     }
544: 
545:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
546:     return ESP_OK;
547: }

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code correctly identifies the core purpose of marking the current firmware version as valid.
- It properly handles the factory partition case by skipping validation, which aligns with the intent in the ground truth.
- However, it misses a critical step: checking the partition state before marking it as valid. The ground truth only marks the app as valid if the state is ESP_OTA_IMG_PENDING_VERIFY, while the generated code unconditionally calls esp_ota_mark_app_valid(). This could lead to incorrect behavior or unnecessary operations.
- Uses esp_ota_mark_app_valid() instead of esp_ota_mark_app_valid_cancel_rollback(), which may have subtly different semantics depending on the ESP-IDF version, though both aim to validate the image.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow is generally similar: get running partition → check for factory → perform action.
- Differences include ordering of log messages (the generated code logs at the start, whereas ground truth logs after determining the partition).
- Missing explicit state checking logic that's central to the original design.
- Error handling patterns are consistent but not identical (both handle errors appropriately).

Functional Completeness:
- Covers main functionality: getting the running partition, identifying factory partitions, and marking the firmware as valid.
- Lacks the verification-state-checking mechanism, which is an important safety feature in the ground truth implementation.
- Includes good error handling and logging throughout, possibly even more verbose than the ground truth.
- Does not use esp_ota_get_state_partition(), which is a key part of the robustness in the reference implementation.

It verifies the integrity of the new firmware header, checks version compatibility, and reports progress and speed during the download process. Upon successful completion, the device can be rebooted to apply the new firmware.

329: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
330: {
331:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
332:     std::stringstream ss(version);
333:     std::string segment;
334: 
335:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
336:     {
337:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
338:     }
339: 
340:     return versionNumbers;
341: }

343: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
344: {
345:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
346:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
347: 
348:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
349:     {
350:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
351:         {
352:             return true;
353:         }
354:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
355:         {
356:             return false;
357:         }
358:     }
359: 
360:     return newer.size() > current.size();
361: }

363: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
364: {
365:     if (!has_serial_number_)
366:     {
367:         return "{}";
368:     }
369: 
370:     std::string hmac_hex;
371: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
372:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
373: 
374:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
375:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
376:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
377:     {
378:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
379:         return "{}";
380:     }
381: 
382:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
383:     {
384:         char buffer[3];
385:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
386:         hmac_hex += buffer;
387:     }
388: #endif
389: 
390:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
391:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
392:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
393:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
394:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
395:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
396:     std::string json(json_str);
397:     cJSON_free(json_str);
398:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
399: 
400:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
401:     return json;
402: }
403: 
404: esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
405: {
406:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
407:     {
408:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
409:         return ESP_FAIL;
410:     }
411: 
412:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
413:     if (url.back() != '/')
414:     {
415:         url += "/activate";
416:     }
417:     else
418:     {
419:         url += "activate";
420:     }
421: 
422:     auto http = SetupHttp();
423: 
424:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
425:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
426: 
427:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
428:     {
429:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
430:         return ESP_FAIL;
431:     }
432: 
433:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
434:     if (status_code == 202)
435:     {
436:         return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
437:     }
438:     if (status_code != 200)
439:     {
440:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
441:         return ESP_FAIL;
442:     }
443: 
444:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
445:     return ESP_OK;
446: }

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code largely mirrors the intended behavior of performing an OTA firmware upgrade over HTTP, including downloading the firmware, writing it to the OTA partition, and setting the boot partition. However, there are some notable semantic differences:
- It does not validate the firmware image header or compare versions like the ground truth.
- Progress/speed calculation is based on total average rather than periodic instantaneous values.
- Uses `OTA_SIZE_UNKNOWN` instead of `OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES`, which may affect performance or compatibility.

Structure Similarity:
While both codes follow a similar high-level workflow (HTTP setup → OTA begin → download/write loop → finalize), the structural details differ significantly:
- Function calls such as `SetupHttp()` vs direct use of `Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork()->CreateHttp(0)` indicate different architectural assumptions.
- Different buffer sizes (4096 vs 512).
- Timing mechanism differs (`esp_timer_get_time()` usage varies).
- Error logging formats and granularity vary.
- Missing explicit handling of image headers and application description checks.

Functional Completeness:
Most core functionality is implemented but lacks several critical safety and validation features found in the ground truth:
- No verification of the incoming firmware's integrity or version checking.
- Lacks robust error recovery paths (e.g., aborting OTA on failure explicitly in multiple places).
- Does not handle partial reads or edge cases during HTTP transfers as comprehensively.
- Callback timing logic differs and might report inaccurate speeds due to cumulative time-based calculation.

results.description: The `Ota::ParseVersion` function takes a version string (e.g., "1.2.3") and splits it into its numeric components, returning them as a vector of integers. This allows for easy comparison and ordering of software versions during OTA update processes.

351:     {
352:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
353:         return false;
354:     }
355: 
356:     char buffer[512];
357:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
358:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
359:     while (true)
360:     {
361:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
362:         if (ret < 0)
363:         {
364:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
365:             return false;
366:         }
367: 
368:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
369:         recent_read += ret;
370:         total_read += ret;
371:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
372:         {
373:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
374:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
375:             if (callback)
376:             {
377:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
378:             }
379:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
380:             recent_read = 0;
381:         }
382: 
383:         if (ret == 0)
384:         {
385:             break;
386:         }
387: 
388:         if (!image_header_checked)
389:         {
390:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
391:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
392:             {
393:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
394:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
395: 
396:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
397:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
398: 
399:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
400:                 {
401:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
402:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
403:                     return false;
404:                 }
405: 
406:                 image_header_checked = true;
407:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
408:             }
409:         }
410:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
411:         if (err != ESP_OK)
412:         {
413:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
414:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
415:             return false;
416:         }
417:     }
418:     http->Close();
419: 
420:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
421:     if (err != ESP_OK)
422:     {
423:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
424:         {
425:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
426:         }
427:         else
428:         {
429:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
430:         }
431:         return false;
432:     }
433: 
434:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
435:     if (err != ESP_OK)
436:     {
437:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
438:         return false;
439:     }
440: 
441:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
442:     return true;
443: }

454: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
455: {
456:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
457:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
458: 
459:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
460:     {
461:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
462:         {
463:             return true;
464:         }
465:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
466:         {
467:             return false;
468:         }
469:     }
470: 
471:     return newer.size() > current.size();
472: }

474: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
475: {
476:     if (!has_serial_number_)
477:     {
478:         return "{}";
479:     }
480: 
481:     std::string hmac_hex;
482: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
483:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
484: 
485:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
486:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
487:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
488:     {
489:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
490:         return "{}";
491:     }
492: 
493:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
494:     {
495:         char buffer[3];
496:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
497:         hmac_hex += buffer;
498:     }
499: #endif
500: 
501:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
502:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
503:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
504:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
505:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
506:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
507:     std::string json(json_str);
508:     cJSON_free(json_str);
509:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
510: 
511:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
512:     return json;
513: }

515: esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
516: {
517:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
518:     {
519:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
520:         return ESP_FAIL;
521:     }
522: 
523:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
524:     if (url.back() != '/')
525:     {
526:         url += "/activate";
527:     }
528:     else
529:     {
530:         url += "activate";
531:     }
532: 
533:     auto http = SetupHttp();
534: 
535:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
536:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
537: 
538:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
539:     {
540:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
541:         return ESP_FAIL;
542:     }
543: 
544:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
545:     if (status_code == 202)
546:     {
547:         return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
548:     }
549:     if (status_code != 200)
550:     {
551:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
552:         return ESP_FAIL;
553:     }
554: 
555:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
556:     return ESP_OK;
557: }
