83: esp_err_t Ota::CheckVersion()
84: {
85:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
86:     auto app_desc = esp_app_get_description();
87: 
88:     // Check if there is a new firmware version available
89:     current_version_ = app_desc->version;
90:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s", current_version_.c_str());
91: 
92:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
93:     if (url.length() < 10)
94:     {
95:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Check version URL is not properly set");
96:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG;
97:     }
98: 
99:     auto http = SetupHttp();
100: 
101:     std::string data = board.GetSystemInfoJson();
102:     std::string method = data.length() > 0 ? "POST" : "GET";
103:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
104: 
105:     if (!http->Open(method, url))
106:     {
107:         int last_error = http->GetLastError();
108:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection, code=0x%x", last_error);
109:         return last_error;
110:     }
111: 
112:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
113:     if (status_code != 200)
114:     {
115:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to check version, status code: %d", status_code);
116:         return status_code;
117:     }
118: 
119:     data = http->ReadAll();
120:     http->Close();
121: 
122:     // Response: { "firmware": { "version": "1.0.0", "url": "http://" } }
123:     // Parse the JSON response and check if the version is newer
124:     // If it is, set has_new_version_ to true and store the new version and URL
125: 
126:     cJSON *root = cJSON_Parse(data.c_str());
127:     if (root == NULL)
128:     {
129:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse JSON response");
130:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_RESPONSE;
131:     }
132: 
133:     has_activation_code_ = false;
134:     has_activation_challenge_ = false;
135:     cJSON *activation = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "activation");
136:     if (cJSON_IsObject(activation))
137:     {
138:         cJSON *message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "message");
139:         if (cJSON_IsString(message))
140:         {
141:             activation_message_ = message->valuestring;
142:         }
143:         cJSON *code = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "code");
144:         if (cJSON_IsString(code))
145:         {
146:             activation_code_ = code->valuestring;
147:             has_activation_code_ = true;
148:         }
149:         cJSON *challenge = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "challenge");
150:         if (cJSON_IsString(challenge))
151:         {
152:             activation_challenge_ = challenge->valuestring;
153:             has_activation_challenge_ = true;
154:         }
155:         cJSON *timeout_ms = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "timeout_ms");
156:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timeout_ms))
157:         {
158:             activation_timeout_ms_ = timeout_ms->valueint;
159:         }
160:     }
161: 
162:     has_mqtt_config_ = false;
163:     cJSON *mqtt = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "mqtt");
164:     if (cJSON_IsObject(mqtt))
165:     {
166:         Settings settings("mqtt", true);
167:         cJSON *item = NULL;
168:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, mqtt)
169:         {
170:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
171:             {
172:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
173:                 {
174:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
175:                 }
176:             }
177:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
178:             {
179:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
180:                 {
181:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
182:                 }
183:             }
184:         }
185:         has_mqtt_config_ = true;
186:     }
187:     else
188:     {
189:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No mqtt section found !");
190:     }
191: 
192:     has_websocket_config_ = false;
193:     cJSON *websocket = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "websocket");
194:     if (cJSON_IsObject(websocket))
195:     {
196:         Settings settings("websocket", true);
197:         cJSON *item = NULL;
198:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, websocket)
199:         {
200:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
201:             {
202:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
203:                 {
204:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
205:                 }
206:             }
207:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
208:             {
209:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
210:                 {
211:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
212:                 }
213:             }
214:         }
215:         has_websocket_config_ = true;
216:     }
217:     else
218:     {
219:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No websocket section found!");
220:     }
221: 
222:     has_server_time_ = false;
223:     cJSON *server_time = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "server_time");
224:     if (cJSON_IsObject(server_time))
225:     {
226:         cJSON *timestamp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timestamp");
227:         cJSON *timezone_offset = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timezone_offset");
228: 
229:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timestamp))
230:         {
231:             // 设置系统时间
232:             struct timeval tv;
233:             double ts = timestamp->valuedouble;
234: 
235:             // 如果有时区偏移，计算本地时间
236:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(timezone_offset))
237:             {
238:                 ts += (timezone_offset->valueint * 60 * 1000); // 转换分钟为毫秒
239:             }
240: 
241:             tv.tv_sec = (time_t)(ts / 1000);                         // 转换毫秒为秒
242:             tv.tv_usec = (suseconds_t)((long long)ts % 1000) * 1000; // 剩余的毫秒转换为微秒
243:             settimeofday(&tv, NULL);
244:             has_server_time_ = true;
245:         }
246:     }
247:     else
248:     {
249:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No server_time section found!");
250:     }
251: 
252:     has_new_version_ = false;
253:     cJSON *firmware = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "firmware");
254:     if (cJSON_IsObject(firmware))
255:     {
256:         cJSON *version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "version");
257:         if (cJSON_IsString(version))
258:         {
259:             firmware_version_ = version->valuestring;
260:         }
261:         cJSON *url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "url");
262:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
263:         {
264:             firmware_url_ = url->valuestring;
265:         }
266: 
267:         if (cJSON_IsString(version) && cJSON_IsString(url))
268:         {
269:             // Check if the version is newer, for example, 0.1.0 is newer than 0.0.1
270:             has_new_version_ = IsNewVersionAvailable(current_version_, firmware_version_);
271:             if (has_new_version_)
272:             {
273:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "New version available: %s", firmware_version_.c_str());
274:             }
275:             else
276:             {
277:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current is the latest version");
278:             }
279:             // If the force flag is set to 1, the given version is forced to be installed
280:             cJSON *force = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "force");
281:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(force) && force->valueint == 1)
282:             {
283:                 has_new_version_ = true;
284:             }
285:         }
286:     }
287:     else
288:     {
289:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No firmware section found!");
290:     }
291: 
292:     cJSON_Delete(root);
293:     return ESP_OK;
294: }

321: {

323:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
324:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
325:     if (update_partition == NULL)

327:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");

332:     bool image_header_checked = false;
333:     std::string image_header;
334: 
335:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
336:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
337:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
338:     {
339:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
340:         return false;

342:

346:         return false;
347:     }

349:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
350:     if (content_length == 0)
351:     {
352:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
353:         return false;
354:     }
355: 
356:     char buffer[512];
357:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
358:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
359:     while (true)
360:     {
361:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
362:         if (ret < 0)
363:         {
364:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
365:             return false;
366:         }
367: 
368:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
369:         recent_read += ret;
370:         total_read += ret;
371:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
372:         {
373:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
374:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
375:             if (callback)
376:             {
377:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
378:             }
379:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
380:             recent_read = 0;
381:         }
382: 
383:         if (ret == 0)
384:         {
385:             break;
386:         }
387: 
388:         if (!image_header_checked)

393:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
394:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
395: 
396:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
397:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
398: 
399:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
400:                 {
401:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
402:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
403:                     return false;
404:                 }
405: 
406:                 image_header_checked = true;
407:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
408:             }
409:         }
410:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
411:         if (err != ESP_OK)
412:         {
413:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
414:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
415:             return false;
416:         }
417:     }
418:     http->Close();
419: 
420:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
421:     if (err != ESP_OK)
422:     {
423:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
424:         {
425:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
426:         }
427:         else
428:         {
429:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
430:         }
431:         return false;
432:     }
433: 
434:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
435:     if (err != ESP_OK)
436:     {
437:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
438:         return false;
439:     }
440: 
441:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
442:     return true;
443: }

468: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
469: {
470:     if (!has_serial_number_)
471:     {
472:         return "{}";
473:     }
474: 
475:     std::string hmac_hex;
476: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
477:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
478: 
479:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
480:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
481:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
482:     {
483:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
484:         return "{}";
485:     }
486: 
487:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
488:     {
489:         char buffer[3];
490:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
491:         hmac_hex += buffer;
492:     }
493: #endif
494: 
495:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
496:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
497:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
498:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
499:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
500:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
501:     std::string json(json_str);
502:     cJSON_free(json_str);
503:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
504: 
505:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
506:     return json;
507: }

509: esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
510: {
511:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
512:     {
513:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
514:         return ESP_FAIL;
515:     }
516: 
517:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
518:     if (url.back() != '/')
519:     {
520:         url += "/activate";
521:     }
522:     else
523:     {
524:         url += "activate";
525:     }
526: 
527:     auto http = SetupHttp();
528: 
529:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
530:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
531: 
532:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
533:     {
534:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
535:         return ESP_FAIL;
536:     }
537: 
538:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
539:     if (status_code == 202)
540:     {
541:         return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
542:     }
543:     if (status_code != 200)
544:     {
545:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
546:         return ESP_FAIL;
547:     }
548: 
549:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
550:     return ESP_OK;
551: }

Semantic Correctness: The generated code perfectly mirrors the logic and behavior of the ground truth. Both implementations parse version strings into integer vectors, compare segments sequentially, and handle cases where one version has more segments than the other. The core algorithm for determining if a new version is available is identical in both versions.

Structure Similarity: The overall structure is very similar but differs slightly in naming conventions and formatting. The function signature matches exactly, as do the control flows and use of standard library functions like `std::min`. However, the generated code uses more descriptive variable names (`currentSegments`, `newSegments`) compared to the ground truth's more concise naming (`current`, `newer`). Additionally, the generated code includes explanatory comments that are absent from the ground truth, which affects structural similarity but not correctness.

Functional Completeness: All key functionalities present in the ground truth are fully covered in the generated code. This includes parsing version strings using `ParseVersion()`, comparing numerical segments iteratively, handling equal segments by continuing comparisons, and correctly resolving cases where versions differ only in the number of segments (e.g., "1.2" vs "1.2.0").

results.description: The `Ota::GetActivationPayload` function generates a JSON-formatted activation payload for device authentication. It includes the device's serial number, a challenge string, and an HMAC-SHA256 signature computed using a hardware-backed key. The payload is returned as a string, or an empty JSON object if the serial number is not available.

83: esp_err_t Ota::CheckVersion()
84: {
85:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
86:     auto app_desc = esp_app_get_description();
87: 
88:     // Check if there is a new firmware version available
89:     current_version_ = app_desc->version;
90:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s", current_version_.c_str());
91: 
92:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
93:     if (url.length() < 10)
94:     {
95:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Check version URL is not properly set");
96:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG;
97:     }
98: 
99:     auto http = SetupHttp();
100: 
101:     std::string data = board.GetSystemInfoJson();
102:     std::string method = data.length() > 0 ? "POST" : "GET";
103:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
104: 
105:     if (!http->Open(method, url))
106:     {
107:         int last_error = http->GetLastError();
108:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection, code=0x%x", last_error);
109:         return last_error;
110:     }
111: 
112:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
113:     if (status_code != 200)
114:     {
115:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to check version, status code: %d", status_code);
116:         return status_code;
117:     }
118: 
119:     data = http->ReadAll();
120:     http->Close();
121: 
122:     // Response: { "firmware": { "version": "1.0.0", "url": "http://" } }
123:     // Parse the JSON response and check if the version is newer
124:     // If it is, set has_new_version_ to true and store the new version and URL
125: 
126:     cJSON *root = cJSON_Parse(data.c_str());
127:     if (root == NULL)
128:     {
129:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse JSON response");
130:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_RESPONSE;
131:     }
132: 
133:     has_activation_code_ = false;
134:     has_activation_challenge_ = false;
135:     cJSON *activation = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "activation");
136:     if (cJSON_IsObject(activation))
137:     {
138:         cJSON *message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "message");
139:         if (cJSON_IsString(message))
140:         {
141:             activation_message_ = message->valuestring;
142:         }
143:         cJSON *code = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "code");
144:         if (cJSON_IsString(code))
145:         {
146:             activation_code_ = code->valuestring;
147:             has_activation_code_ = true;
148:         }
149:         cJSON *challenge = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "challenge");
150:         if (cJSON_IsString(challenge))
151:         {
152:             activation_challenge_ = challenge->valuestring;
153:             has_activation_challenge_ = true;
154:         }
155:         cJSON *timeout_ms = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "timeout_ms");
156:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timeout_ms))
157:         {
158:             activation_timeout_ms_ = timeout_ms->valueint;
159:         }
160:     }
161: 
162:     has_mqtt_config_ = false;
163:     cJSON *mqtt = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "mqtt");
164:     if (cJSON_IsObject(mqtt))
165:     {
166:         Settings settings("mqtt", true);
167:         cJSON *item = NULL;
168:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, mqtt)
169:         {
170:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
171:             {
172:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
173:                 {
174:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
175:                 }
176:             }
177:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
178:             {
179:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
180:                 {
181:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
182:                 }
183:             }
184:         }
185:         has_mqtt_config_ = true;
186:     }
187:     else
188:     {
189:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No mqtt section found !");
190:     }
191: 
192:     has_websocket_config_ = false;
193:     cJSON *websocket = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "websocket");
194:     if (cJSON_IsObject(websocket))
195:     {
196:         Settings settings("websocket", true);
197:         cJSON *item = NULL;
198:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, websocket)
199:         {
200:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
201:             {
202:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
203:                 {
204:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
205:                 }
206:             }
207:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
208:             {
209:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
210:                 {
211:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
212:                 }
213:             }
214:         }
215:         has_websocket_config_ = true;
216:     }
217:     else
218:     {
219:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No websocket section found!");
220:     }
221: 
222:     has_server_time_ = false;
223:     cJSON *server_time = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "server_time");
224:     if (cJSON_IsObject(server_time))
225:     {
226:         cJSON *timestamp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timestamp");
227:         cJSON *timezone_offset = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timezone_offset");
228: 
229:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timestamp))
230:         {
231:             // 设置系统时间
232:             struct timeval tv;
233:             double ts = timestamp->valuedouble;
234: 
235:             // 如果有时区偏移，计算本地时间
236:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(timezone_offset))
237:             {
238:                 ts += (timezone_offset->valueint * 60 * 1000); // 转换分钟为毫秒
239:             }
240: 
241:             tv.tv_sec = (time_t)(ts / 1000);                         // 转换毫秒为秒
242:             tv.tv_usec = (suseconds_t)((long long)ts % 1000) * 1000; // 剩余的毫秒转换为微秒
243:             settimeofday(&tv, NULL);
244:             has_server_time_ = true;
245:         }
246:     }
247:     else
248:     {
249:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No server_time section found!");
250:     }
251: 
252:     has_new_version_ = false;
253:     cJSON *firmware = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "firmware");
254:     if (cJSON_IsObject(firmware))
255:     {
256:         cJSON *version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "version");
257:         if (cJSON_IsString(version))
258:         {
259:             firmware_version_ = version->valuestring;
260:         }
261:         cJSON *url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "url");
262:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
263:         {
264:             firmware_url_ = url->valuestring;
265:         }
266: 
267:         if (cJSON_IsString(version) && cJSON_IsString(url))
268:         {
269:             // Check if the version is newer, for example, 0.1.0 is newer than 0.0.1
270:             has_new_version_ = IsNewVersionAvailable(current_version_, firmware_version_);
271:             if (has_new_version_)
272:             {
273:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "New version available: %s", firmware_version_.c_str());
274:             }
275:             else
276:             {
277:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current is the latest version");
278:             }
279:             // If the force flag is set to 1, the given version is forced to be installed
280:             cJSON *force = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "force");
281:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(force) && force->valueint == 1)
282:             {
283:                 has_new_version_ = true;
284:             }
285:         }
286:     }
287:     else
288:     {
289:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No firmware section found!");
290:     }
291: 
292:     cJSON_Delete(root);
293:     return ESP_OK;
294: }

320: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
321: {
322:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
323:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
324:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
325:     if (update_partition == NULL)
326:     {
327:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
328:         return false;
329:     }
330: 
331:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition %s at offset 0x%lx", update_partition->label, update_partition->address);
332:     bool image_header_checked = false;
333:     std::string image_header;
334: 
335:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
336:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
337:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
338:     {
339:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
340:         return false;
341:     }
342: 
343:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
344:     {
345:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
346:         return false;
347:     }
348: 
349:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
350:     if (content_length == 0)
351:     {
352:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
353:         return false;
354:     }
355: 
356:     char buffer[512];
357:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
358:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
359:     while (true)
360:     {
361:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
362:         if (ret < 0)
363:         {
364:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
365:             return false;
366:         }
367: 
368:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
369:         recent_read += ret;
370:         total_read += ret;
371:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
372:         {
373:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
374:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
375:             if (callback)
376:             {
377:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
378:             }
379:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
380:             recent_read = 0;
381:         }
382: 
383:         if (ret == 0)
384:         {
385:             break;
386:         }
387: 
388:         if (!image_header_checked)
389:         {
390:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
391:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
392:             {
393:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
394:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
395: 
396:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
397:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
398: 
399:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
400:                 {
401:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
402:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
403:                     return false;
404:                 }
405: 
406:                 image_header_checked = true;
407:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
408:             }
409:         }
410:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
411:         if (err != ESP_OK)
412:         {
413:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
414:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
415:             return false;
416:         }
417:     }
418:     http->Close();
419: 
420:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
421:     if (err != ESP_OK)
422:     {
423:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
424:         {
425:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
426:         }
427:         else
428:         {
429:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
430:         }
431:         return false;
432:     }
433: 
434:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
435:     if (err != ESP_OK)
436:     {
437:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
438:         return false;
439:     }
440: 
441:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
442:     return true;
443: }

454:     std::string segment;
455:

464: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
465: {
466:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
467:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
468: 
469:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
470:     {
471:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
472:         {
473:             return true;
474:         }
475:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
476:         {
477:             return false;
478:         }
479:     }
480: 
481:     return newer.size() > current.size();

Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement the same overall functionality but diverges significantly in how HMAC is computed.
- Ground truth uses `esp_hmac_calculate` with a predefined key (`HMAC_KEY0`) directly on the challenge data.
- Generated code incorrectly constructs a message from serial number and challenge, then uses a different HMAC API (`esp_hmac_start`, `update`, `finish`) which may not align with expected behavior or security practices.
- Use of `has_activation_challenge_` flag in generated code is not present in ground truth, suggesting potential divergence in logic handling.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches, and both use cJSON for JSON construction.
- Both handle the case where there's no serial number by returning "{}".
- However, control flow differs: ground truth computes HMAC first (conditionally), then builds JSON; generated builds JSON progressively.
- Different cJSON printing functions used (`PrintUnformatted` vs `Print`), affecting output formatting.
- Memory management differs slightly in cleanup phase.

Functional Completeness:
- Both codes check for presence of serial number.
- Both attempt to generate an HMAC signature if supported.
- Generated code includes additional conditional logic for challenge existence that’s absent in ground truth.
- Missing elements in generated code include logging via `ESP_LOGI`, explicit algorithm field ("hmac-sha256"), and proper error logging using `ESP_LOGE`.
- The HMAC computation method does not match the ground truth, potentially leading to incorrect results.

83: esp_err_t Ota::CheckVersion()
84: {
85:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
86:     auto app_desc = esp_app_get_description();
87: 
88:     // Check if there is a new firmware version available
89:     current_version_ = app_desc->version;
90:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s", current_version_.c_str());
91: 
92:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
93:     if (url.length() < 10)
94:     {
95:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Check version URL is not properly set");
96:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG;
97:     }
98: 
99:     auto http = SetupHttp();
100: 
101:     std::string data = board.GetSystemInfoJson();
102:     std::string method = data.length() > 0 ? "POST" : "GET";
103:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
104: 
105:     if (!http->Open(method, url))
106:     {
107:         int last_error = http->GetLastError();
108:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection, code=0x%x", last_error);
109:         return last_error;
110:     }
111: 
112:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
113:     if (status_code != 200)
114:     {
115:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to check version, status code: %d", status_code);
116:         return status_code;
117:     }
118: 
119:     data = http->ReadAll();
120:     http->Close();
121: 
122:     // Response: { "firmware": { "version": "1.0.0", "url": "http://" } }
123:     // Parse the JSON response and check if the version is newer
124:     // If it is, set has_new_version_ to true and store the new version and URL
125: 
126:     cJSON *root = cJSON_Parse(data.c_str());
127:     if (root == NULL)
128:     {
129:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse JSON response");
130:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_RESPONSE;
131:     }
132: 
133:     has_activation_code_ = false;
134:     has_activation_challenge_ = false;
135:     cJSON *activation = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "activation");
136:     if (cJSON_IsObject(activation))
137:     {
138:         cJSON *message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "message");
139:         if (cJSON_IsString(message))
140:         {
141:             activation_message_ = message->valuestring;
142:         }
143:         cJSON *code = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "code");
144:         if (cJSON_IsString(code))
145:         {
146:             activation_code_ = code->valuestring;
147:             has_activation_code_ = true;
148:         }
149:         cJSON *challenge = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "challenge");
150:         if (cJSON_IsString(challenge))
151:         {
152:             activation_challenge_ = challenge->valuestring;
153:             has_activation_challenge_ = true;
154:         }
155:         cJSON *timeout_ms = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "timeout_ms");
156:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timeout_ms))
157:         {
158:             activation_timeout_ms_ = timeout_ms->valueint;
159:         }
160:     }
161: 
162:     has_mqtt_config_ = false;
163:     cJSON *mqtt = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "mqtt");
164:     if (cJSON_IsObject(mqtt))
165:     {
166:         Settings settings("mqtt", true);
167:         cJSON *item = NULL;
168:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, mqtt)
169:         {
170:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
171:             {
172:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
173:                 {
174:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
175:                 }
176:             }
177:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
178:             {
179:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
180:                 {
181:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
182:                 }
183:             }
184:         }
185:         has_mqtt_config_ = true;
186:     }
187:     else
188:     {
189:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No mqtt section found !");
190:     }
191: 
192:     has_websocket_config_ = false;
193:     cJSON *websocket = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "websocket");
194:     if (cJSON_IsObject(websocket))
195:     {
196:         Settings settings("websocket", true);
197:         cJSON *item = NULL;
198:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, websocket)
199:         {
200:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
201:             {
202:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
203:                 {
204:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
205:                 }
206:             }
207:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
208:             {
209:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
210:                 {
211:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
212:                 }
213:             }
214:         }
215:         has_websocket_config_ = true;
216:     }
217:     else
218:     {
219:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No websocket section found!");
220:     }
221: 
222:     has_server_time_ = false;
223:     cJSON *server_time = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "server_time");
224:     if (cJSON_IsObject(server_time))
225:     {
226:         cJSON *timestamp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timestamp");
227:         cJSON *timezone_offset = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timezone_offset");
228: 
229:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timestamp))
230:         {
231:             // 设置系统时间
232:             struct timeval tv;
233:             double ts = timestamp->valuedouble;
234: 
235:             // 如果有时区偏移，计算本地时间
236:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(timezone_offset))
237:             {
238:                 ts += (timezone_offset->valueint * 60 * 1000); // 转换分钟为毫秒
239:             }
240: 
241:             tv.tv_sec = (time_t)(ts / 1000);                         // 转换毫秒为秒
242:             tv.tv_usec = (suseconds_t)((long long)ts % 1000) * 1000; // 剩余的毫秒转换为微秒
243:             settimeofday(&tv, NULL);
244:             has_server_time_ = true;
245:         }
246:     }
247:     else
248:     {
249:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No server_time section found!");
250:     }
251: 
252:     has_new_version_ = false;
253:     cJSON *firmware = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "firmware");
254:     if (cJSON_IsObject(firmware))
255:     {
256:         cJSON *version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "version");
257:         if (cJSON_IsString(version))
258:         {
259:             firmware_version_ = version->valuestring;
260:         }
261:         cJSON *url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "url");
262:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
263:         {
264:             firmware_url_ = url->valuestring;
265:         }
266: 
267:         if (cJSON_IsString(version) && cJSON_IsString(url))
268:         {
269:             // Check if the version is newer, for example, 0.1.0 is newer than 0.0.1
270:             has_new_version_ = IsNewVersionAvailable(current_version_, firmware_version_);
271:             if (has_new_version_)
272:             {
273:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "New version available: %s", firmware_version_.c_str());
274:             }
275:             else
276:             {
277:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current is the latest version");
278:             }
279:             // If the force flag is set to 1, the given version is forced to be installed
280:             cJSON *force = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "force");
281:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(force) && force->valueint == 1)
282:             {
283:                 has_new_version_ = true;
284:             }
285:         }
286:     }
287:     else
288:     {
289:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No firmware section found!");
290:     }
291: 
292:     cJSON_Delete(root);
293:     return ESP_OK;
294: }

296: void Ota::MarkCurrentVersionValid()
297: {
298:     auto partition = esp_ota_get_running_partition();
299:     if (strcmp(partition->label, "factory") == 0)
300:     {
301:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Running from factory partition, skipping");
302:         return;
303:     }
304: 
305:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Running partition: %s", partition->label);
306:     esp_ota_img_states_t state;
307:     if (esp_ota_get_state_partition(partition, &state) != ESP_OK)
308:     {
309:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get state of partition");
310:         return;
311:     }
312: 
313:     if (state == ESP_OTA_IMG_PENDING_VERIFY)
314:     {
315:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Marking firmware as valid");
316:         esp_ota_mark_app_valid_cancel_rollback();
317:     }
318: }

320: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
321: {
322:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
323:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
324:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
325:     if (update_partition == NULL)
326:     {
327:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
328:         return false;
329:     }
330: 
331:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition %s at offset 0x%lx", update_partition->label, update_partition->address);
332:     bool image_header_checked = false;
333:     std::string image_header;
334: 
335:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
336:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
337:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
338:     {
339:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
340:         return false;
341:     }
342: 
343:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
344:     {
345:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
346:         return false;
347:     }
348: 
349:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
350:     if (content_length == 0)
351:     {
352:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
353:         return false;
354:     }
355: 
356:     char buffer[512];
357:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
358:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
359:     while (true)
360:     {
361:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
362:         if (ret < 0)
363:         {
364:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
365:             return false;
366:         }
367: 
368:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
369:         recent_read += ret;
370:         total_read += ret;
371:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
372:         {
373:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
374:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
375:             if (callback)
376:             {
377:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
378:             }
379:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
380:             recent_read = 0;
381:         }
382: 
383:         if (ret == 0)
384:         {
385:             break;
386:         }
387: 
388:         if (!image_header_checked)
389:         {
390:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
391:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
392:             {
393:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
394:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
395: 
396:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
397:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
398: 
399:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
400:                 {
401:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
402:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
403:                     return false;
404:                 }
405: 
406:                 image_header_checked = true;
407:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
408:             }
409:         }
410:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
411:         if (err != ESP_OK)
412:         {
413:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
414:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
415:             return false;
416:         }
417:     }
418:     http->Close();
419: 
420:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
421:     if (err != ESP_OK)
422:     {
423:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
424:         {
425:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
426:         }
427:         else
428:         {
429:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
430:         }
431:         return false;
432:     }
433: 
434:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
435:     if (err != ESP_OK)
436:     {
437:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
438:         return false;
439:     }
440: 
441:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
442:     return true;
443: }

450: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
451: {
452:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
453:     std::stringstream ss(version);
454:     std::string segment;
455: 
456:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
457:     {
458:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
459:     }
460: 
461:     return versionNumbers;
462: }
463: 
464: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
465: {
466:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
467:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
468: 
469:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
470:     {
471:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
472:         {
473:             return true;
474:         }
475:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
476:         {
477:             return false;
478:         }
479:     }
480: 
481:     return newer.size() > current.size();
482: }

484: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
485: {
486:     if (!has_serial_number_)
487:     {
488:         return "{}";
489:     }
490: 
491:     std::string hmac_hex;
492: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
493:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
494: 
495:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
496:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
497:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
498:     {
499:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
500:         return "{}";
501:     }
502: 
503:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
504:     {
505:         char buffer[3];
506:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
507:         hmac_hex += buffer;
508:     }
509: #endif
510: 
511:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
512:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
513:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
514:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
515:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
516:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
517:     std::string json(json_str);
518:     cJSON_free(json_str);
519:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
520: 
521:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());

1: #include "settings.h"
2: 
3: #include <esp_log.h>
4: #include <nvs_flash.h>
5: 
6: #define TAG "Settings"
7: 
8: Settings::Settings(const std::string &ns, bool read_write) : ns_(ns), read_write_(read_write)
9: {
10:     nvs_open(ns.c_str(), read_write_ ? NVS_READWRITE : NVS_READONLY, &nvs_handle_);
11: }
12: 
13: Settings::~Settings()
14: {
15: }
16: 
17: std::string Settings::GetString(const std::string &key, const std::string &default_value)
18: {
19:     if (nvs_handle_ == 0)
20:     {
21:         return default_value;
22:     }
23: 
24:     size_t length = 0;
25:     if (nvs_get_str(nvs_handle_, key.c_str(), nullptr, &length) != ESP_OK)
26:     {
27:         return default_value;
28:     }
29: 
30:     std::string value;
31:     value.resize(length);
32:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(nvs_get_str(nvs_handle_, key.c_str(), value.data(), &length));
33:     while (!value.empty() && value.back() == '\0')
34:     {
35:         value.pop_back();
36:     }
37:     return value;
38: }
39: 
40: void Settings::SetString(const std::string &key, const std::string &value)
41: {
42:     if (read_write_)
43:     {
44:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(nvs_set_str(nvs_handle_, key.c_str(), value.c_str()));
45:         dirty_ = true;
46:     }
47:     else
48:     {
49:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Namespace %s is not open for writing", ns_.c_str());
50:     }
51: }
52:

54: {
55:     if (nvs_handle_ == 0)
56:     {
57:         return default_value;
58:     }
59: 
60:     int32_t value;
61:     if (nvs_get_i32(nvs_handle_, key.c_str(), &value) != ESP_OK)
62:     {
63:         return default_value;
64:     }
65:     return value;
66: }
67: 
68: void Settings::SetInt(const std::string &key, int32_t value)
69: {
70:     if (read_write_)

92:     }

96: void Settings::SetBool(const std::string &key, bool value)
97: {
98:     if (read_write_)
99:     {
100:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(nvs_set_u8(nvs_handle_, key.c_str(), value ? 1 : 0));
101:         dirty_ = true;
102:     }
103:     else

105:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Namespace %s is not open for writing", ns_.c_str());

118:     }

130:     }

134:     }
135: }

8: Settings::Settings(const std::string &ns, bool read_write) : ns_(ns), read_write_(read_write)
9: {
10:     nvs_open(ns.c_str(), read_write_ ? NVS_READWRITE : NVS_READONLY, &nvs_handle_);
11: }
12: 
13: Settings::~Settings()
14: {
15:     if (nvs_handle_ != 0)
16:     {
17:         if (read_write_ && dirty_)
18:         {
19:             ESP_ERROR_CHECK(nvs_commit(nvs_handle_));
20:         }
21:         nvs_close(nvs_handle_);
22:     }
23: }
24: 
25: std::string Settings::GetString(const std::string &key, const std::string &default_value)
26: {
27:     if (nvs_handle_ == 0)
28:     {
29:         return default_value;
30:     }
31: 
32:     size_t length = 0;
33:     if (nvs_get_str(nvs_handle_, key.c_str(), nullptr, &length) != ESP_OK)
34:     {
35:         return default_value;
36:     }
37: 
38:     std::string value;
39:     value.resize(length);
40:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(nvs_get_str(nvs_handle_, key.c_str(), value.data(), &length));
41:     while (!value.empty() && value.back() == '\0')
42:     {
43:         value.pop_back();
44:     }
45:     return value;
46: }
47: 
48: void Settings::SetString(const std::string &key, const std::string &value)
49: {
50:     if (read_write_)
51:     {
52:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(nvs_set_str(nvs_handle_, key.c_str(), value.c_str()));
53:         dirty_ = true;
54:     }
55:     else
56:     {
57:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Namespace %s is not open for writing", ns_.c_str());
58:     }
59: }

56: esp_err_t SystemInfo::PrintTaskCpuUsage(TickType_t xTicksToWait)
57: {
58: #define ARRAY_SIZE_OFFSET 5
59:     TaskStatus_t *start_array = NULL, *end_array = NULL;
60:     UBaseType_t start_array_size, end_array_size;
61:     configRUN_TIME_COUNTER_TYPE start_run_time, end_run_time;
62:     esp_err_t ret;
63:     uint32_t total_elapsed_time;
64: 
65:     // Allocate array to store current task states
66:     start_array_size = uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks() + ARRAY_SIZE_OFFSET;
67:     start_array = (TaskStatus_t *)malloc(sizeof(TaskStatus_t) * start_array_size);
68:     if (start_array == NULL)
69:     {
70:         ret = ESP_ERR_NO_MEM;
71:         goto exit;
72:     }
73:     // Get current task states
74:     start_array_size = uxTaskGetSystemState(start_array, start_array_size, &start_run_time);
75:     if (start_array_size == 0)
76:     {
77:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_SIZE;
78:         goto exit;
79:     }
80: 
81:     vTaskDelay(xTicksToWait);
82: 
83:     // Allocate array to store tasks states post delay
84:     end_array_size = uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks() + ARRAY_SIZE_OFFSET;
85:     end_array = (TaskStatus_t *)malloc(sizeof(TaskStatus_t) * end_array_size);
86:     if (end_array == NULL)
87:     {
88:         ret = ESP_ERR_NO_MEM;
89:         goto exit;
90:     }
91:     // Get post delay task states
92:     end_array_size = uxTaskGetSystemState(end_array, end_array_size, &end_run_time);
93:     if (end_array_size == 0)
94:     {
95:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_SIZE;
96:         goto exit;
97:     }
98: 
99:     // Calculate total_elapsed_time in units of run time stats clock period.
100:     total_elapsed_time = (end_run_time - start_run_time);
101:     if (total_elapsed_time == 0)
102:     {
103:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
104:         goto exit;
105:     }
106: 
107:     printf("| Task | Run Time | Percentage\n");
108:     // Match each task in start_array to those in the end_array
109:     for (int i = 0; i < start_array_size; i++)
110:     {
111:         int k = -1;
112:         for (int j = 0; j < end_array_size; j++)
113:         {
114:             if (start_array[i].xHandle == end_array[j].xHandle)
115:             {
116:                 k = j;
117:                 // Mark that task have been matched by overwriting their handles
118:                 start_array[i].xHandle = NULL;
119:                 end_array[j].xHandle = NULL;
120:                 break;
121:             }
122:         }
123:         // Check if matching task found
124:         if (k >= 0)
125:         {
126:             uint32_t task_elapsed_time = end_array[k].ulRunTimeCounter - start_array[i].ulRunTimeCounter;
127:             uint32_t percentage_time = (task_elapsed_time * 100UL) / (total_elapsed_time * CONFIG_FREERTOS_NUMBER_OF_CORES);
128:             printf("| %-16s | %8lu | %4lu%%\n", start_array[i].pcTaskName, task_elapsed_time, percentage_time);
129:         }
130:     }
131: 
132:     // Print unmatched tasks
133:     for (int i = 0; i < start_array_size; i++)
134:     {
135:         if (start_array[i].xHandle != NULL)
136:         {
137:             printf("| %s | Deleted\n", start_array[i].pcTaskName);
138:         }
139:     }
140:     for (int i = 0; i < end_array_size; i++)
141:     {
142:         if (end_array[i].xHandle != NULL)
143:         {
144:             printf("| %s | Created\n", end_array[i].pcTaskName);
145:         }
146:     }
147:     ret = ESP_OK;
148: 
149: exit: // Common return path
150:     free(start_array);
151:     free(end_array);
152:     return ret;
153: }
154: 
155: void SystemInfo::PrintTaskList()
156: {
157:     char buffer[1000];
158:     vTaskList(buffer);
159:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Task list: \n%s", buffer);
160: }

163: {
164:     int free_sram = heap_caps_get_free_size(MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL);
165:     int min_free_sram = heap_caps_get_minimum_free_size(MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL);
166:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "free sram: %u minimal sram: %u", free_sram, min_free_sram);
167: }

60: esp_err_t SystemInfo::PrintTaskCpuUsage(TickType_t xTicksToWait)
61: {
62: #define ARRAY_SIZE_OFFSET 5
63:     TaskStatus_t *start_array = NULL, *end_array = NULL;
64:     UBaseType_t start_array_size, end_array_size;
65:     configRUN_TIME_COUNTER_TYPE start_run_time, end_run_time;
66:     esp_err_t ret;
67:     uint32_t total_elapsed_time;
68: 
69:     // Allocate array to store current task states
70:     start_array_size = uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks() + ARRAY_SIZE_OFFSET;
71:     start_array = (TaskStatus_t *)malloc(sizeof(TaskStatus_t) * start_array_size);
72:     if (start_array == NULL)
73:     {
74:         ret = ESP_ERR_NO_MEM;
75:         goto exit;
76:     }
77:     // Get current task states
78:     start_array_size = uxTaskGetSystemState(start_array, start_array_size, &start_run_time);
79:     if (start_array_size == 0)
80:     {
81:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_SIZE;
82:         goto exit;
83:     }
84: 
85:     vTaskDelay(xTicksToWait);
86: 
87:     // Allocate array to store tasks states post delay
88:     end_array_size = uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks() + ARRAY_SIZE_OFFSET;
89:     end_array = (TaskStatus_t *)malloc(sizeof(TaskStatus_t) * end_array_size);
90:     if (end_array == NULL)
91:     {
92:         ret = ESP_ERR_NO_MEM;
93:         goto exit;
94:     }
95:     // Get post delay task states
96:     end_array_size = uxTaskGetSystemState(end_array, end_array_size, &end_run_time);
97:     if (end_array_size == 0)
98:     {
99:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_SIZE;
100:         goto exit;
101:     }
102: 
103:     // Calculate total_elapsed_time in units of run time stats clock period.
104:     total_elapsed_time = (end_run_time - start_run_time);
105:     if (total_elapsed_time == 0)
106:     {
107:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
108:         goto exit;
109:     }
110: 
111:     printf("| Task | Run Time | Percentage\n");
112:     // Match each task in start_array to those in the end_array
113:     for (int i = 0; i < start_array_size; i++)
114:     {
115:         int k = -1;
116:         for (int j = 0; j < end_array_size; j++)
117:         {
118:             if (start_array[i].xHandle == end_array[j].xHandle)
119:             {
120:                 k = j;
121:                 // Mark that task have been matched by overwriting their handles
122:                 start_array[i].xHandle = NULL;
123:                 end_array[j].xHandle = NULL;
124:                 break;
125:             }
126:         }
127:         // Check if matching task found
128:         if (k >= 0)
129:         {
130:             uint32_t task_elapsed_time = end_array[k].ulRunTimeCounter - start_array[i].ulRunTimeCounter;
131:             uint32_t percentage_time = (task_elapsed_time * 100UL) / (total_elapsed_time * CONFIG_FREERTOS_NUMBER_OF_CORES);
132:             printf("| %-16s | %8lu | %4lu%%\n", start_array[i].pcTaskName, task_elapsed_time, percentage_time);
133:         }
134:     }
135: 
136:     // Print unmatched tasks
137:     for (int i = 0; i < start_array_size; i++)
138:     {
139:         if (start_array[i].xHandle != NULL)
140:         {
141:             printf("| %s | Deleted\n", start_array[i].pcTaskName);
142:         }
143:     }
144:     for (int i = 0; i < end_array_size; i++)
145:     {
146:         if (end_array[i].xHandle != NULL)
147:         {
148:             printf("| %s | Created\n", end_array[i].pcTaskName);
149:         }
150:     }
151:     ret = ESP_OK;
152: 
153: exit: // Common return path
154:     free(start_array);
155:     free(end_array);
156:     return ret;
157: }
158:

160: {
161:     char buffer[1000];
162:     vTaskList(buffer);
163:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Task list: \n%s", buffer);
164: }

167: {
168:     int free_sram = heap_caps_get_free_size(MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL);
169:     int min_free_sram = heap_caps_get_minimum_free_size(MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL);
170:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "free sram: %u minimal sram: %u", free_sram, min_free_sram);
171: }
172:

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (30/100): The generated code does not preserve the core logic of the ground truth. While both functions aim to print task CPU usage, the ground truth implements a detailed calculation of CPU percentages by capturing task states before and after a delay, then computing the difference in runtime counters. In contrast, the generated code simply calls `vTaskGetRunTimeStats` twice but only prints the second result, completely missing the comparative analysis and percentage calculation. Additionally, it uses `ESP_LOG` instead of `printf`, which changes the output mechanism.

Structure Similarity (40/100): There is some similarity in high-level intent and use of FreeRTOS APIs like `vTaskDelay`, but the overall structure diverges significantly. The ground truth manually manages memory for task status arrays, performs system state captures, matches tasks across snapshots, and handles edge cases (e.g., created/deleted tasks). The generated code takes a shortcut using `vTaskGetRunTimeStats`, which simplifies the implementation but omits critical structural components such as dual sampling, manual task matching, and dynamic memory management with error handling via goto.

Functional Completeness (25/100): The generated code lacks most of the essential functionality present in the ground truth. It misses:
 - Dual snapshot collection of task states
 - Manual calculation of CPU usage percentages
 - Matching of tasks between snapshots
 - Reporting of newly created or deleted tasks
 - Proper error handling and resource cleanup beyond basic deallocation
 - Use of correct data types and constants like `CONFIG_FREERTOS_NUMBER_OF_CORES`
 - Equivalent output formatting

The generated version also introduces platform-specific assumptions (like `ESP_LOG` and `TAG`) that are not part of the original specification, reducing portability and fidelity to the reference implementation.

63: esp_err_t SystemInfo::PrintTaskCpuUsage(TickType_t xTicksToWait)
64: {
65: #define ARRAY_SIZE_OFFSET 5
66:     TaskStatus_t *start_array = NULL, *end_array = NULL;
67:     UBaseType_t start_array_size, end_array_size;
68:     configRUN_TIME_COUNTER_TYPE start_run_time, end_run_time;
69:     esp_err_t ret;
70:     uint32_t total_elapsed_time;
71: 
72:     // Allocate array to store current task states
73:     start_array_size = uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks() + ARRAY_SIZE_OFFSET;
74:     start_array = (TaskStatus_t *)malloc(sizeof(TaskStatus_t) * start_array_size);
75:     if (start_array == NULL)
76:     {
77:         ret = ESP_ERR_NO_MEM;
78:         goto exit;
79:     }
80:     // Get current task states
81:     start_array_size = uxTaskGetSystemState(start_array, start_array_size, &start_run_time);
82:     if (start_array_size == 0)
83:     {
84:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_SIZE;
85:         goto exit;
86:     }
87: 
88:     vTaskDelay(xTicksToWait);
89: 
90:     // Allocate array to store tasks states post delay
91:     end_array_size = uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks() + ARRAY_SIZE_OFFSET;
92:     end_array = (TaskStatus_t *)malloc(sizeof(TaskStatus_t) * end_array_size);
93:     if (end_array == NULL)
94:     {
95:         ret = ESP_ERR_NO_MEM;
96:         goto exit;
97:     }
98:     // Get post delay task states
99:     end_array_size = uxTaskGetSystemState(end_array, end_array_size, &end_run_time);
100:     if (end_array_size == 0)
101:     {
102:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_SIZE;
103:         goto exit;
104:     }
105: 
106:     // Calculate total_elapsed_time in units of run time stats clock period.
107:     total_elapsed_time = (end_run_time - start_run_time);
108:     if (total_elapsed_time == 0)
109:     {
110:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
111:         goto exit;
112:     }
113: 
114:     printf("| Task | Run Time | Percentage\n");
115:     // Match each task in start_array to those in the end_array
116:     for (int i = 0; i < start_array_size; i++)
117:     {
118:         int k = -1;
119:         for (int j = 0; j < end_array_size; j++)
120:         {
121:             if (start_array[i].xHandle == end_array[j].xHandle)
122:             {
123:                 k = j;
124:                 // Mark that task have been matched by overwriting their handles
125:                 start_array[i].xHandle = NULL;
126:                 end_array[j].xHandle = NULL;
127:                 break;
128:             }
129:         }
130:         // Check if matching task found
131:         if (k >= 0)
132:         {
133:             uint32_t task_elapsed_time = end_array[k].ulRunTimeCounter - start_array[i].ulRunTimeCounter;
134:             uint32_t percentage_time = (task_elapsed_time * 100UL) / (total_elapsed_time * CONFIG_FREERTOS_NUMBER_OF_CORES);
135:             printf("| %-16s | %8lu | %4lu%%\n", start_array[i].pcTaskName, task_elapsed_time, percentage_time);
136:         }
137:     }
138: 
139:     // Print unmatched tasks
140:     for (int i = 0; i < start_array_size; i++)
141:     {
142:         if (start_array[i].xHandle != NULL)
143:         {
144:             printf("| %s | Deleted\n", start_array[i].pcTaskName);
145:         }
146:     }
147:     for (int i = 0; i < end_array_size; i++)
148:     {
149:         if (end_array[i].xHandle != NULL)
150:         {
151:             printf("| %s | Created\n", end_array[i].pcTaskName);
152:         }
153:     }
154:     ret = ESP_OK;
155: 
156: exit: // Common return path
157:     free(start_array);
158:     free(end_array);
159:     return ret;
160: }

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (85): The generated code has very similar semantics to the ground truth. Both functions check for valid data processing and return boolean values accordingly. However, there's a subtle difference in the success condition - ground truth returns true only when samples > 0, while generated code returns true when samples_read >= 0. This means the generated code would return true even when no samples are read (samples_read = 0), which could be semantically different depending on the intended behavior of the Read() function.

Structure Similarity (75): The function signatures are nearly identical with minor formatting differences (spacing around &). Both use the same parameter name and return type. The control flow is similar but structured differently - ground truth uses a simple if-else pattern while generated code uses early return and direct boolean evaluation. The API calls are the same (Read function with data.data() and data.size()), but the generated code adds an extra empty check that's not present in the ground truth.

Functional Completeness (80): The generated code covers the core functionality of reading data and returning success status. It includes an additional safety check for empty data which could be seen as an enhancement. However, it doesn't exactly replicate the ground truth's logic for determining success/failure conditions. The generated code handles the edge case of empty input but changes the fundamental success criteria from "samples > 0" to "samples_read >= 0".

3: #include <esp_log.h>

8: }

10: Es8388AudioCodec::~Es8388AudioCodec()
11: {
12:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(output_dev_));
13:     esp_codec_dev_delete(output_dev_);
14:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(input_dev_));
15:     esp_codec_dev_delete(input_dev_);
16: 
17:     audio_codec_delete_codec_if(codec_if_);
18:     audio_codec_delete_ctrl_if(ctrl_if_);

20:     audio_codec_delete_data_if(data_if_);

23: void Es8388AudioCodec::CreateDuplexChannels(gpio_num_t mclk, gpio_num_t bclk, gpio_num_t ws, gpio_num_t dout, gpio_num_t din)
24: {
25:     assert(input_sample_rate_ == output_sample_rate_);
26: 
27:     i2s_chan_config_t chan_cfg = {
28:         .id = I2S_NUM_0,
29:         .role = I2S_ROLE_MASTER,
30:         .dma_desc_num = AUDIO_CODEC_DMA_DESC_NUM,
31:         .dma_frame_num = AUDIO_CODEC_DMA_FRAME_NUM,
32:         .auto_clear_after_cb = true,
33:         .auto_clear_before_cb = false,
34:         .intr_priority = 0,
35:     };
36:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(i2s_new_channel(&chan_cfg, &tx_handle_, &rx_handle_));
37: 
38:     i2s_std_config_t std_cfg = {
39:         .clk_cfg = {
40:             .sample_rate_hz = (uint32_t)output_sample_rate_,
41:             .clk_src = I2S_CLK_SRC_DEFAULT,
42:             .ext_clk_freq_hz = 0,
43:             .mclk_multiple = I2S_MCLK_MULTIPLE_256},
44:         .slot_cfg = {.data_bit_width = I2S_DATA_BIT_WIDTH_16BIT, .slot_bit_width = I2S_SLOT_BIT_WIDTH_AUTO, .slot_mode = I2S_SLOT_MODE_STEREO, .slot_mask = I2S_STD_SLOT_BOTH, .ws_width = I2S_DATA_BIT_WIDTH_16BIT, .ws_pol = false, .bit_shift = true, .left_align = true, .big_endian = false, .bit_order_lsb = false},
45:         .gpio_cfg = {.mclk = mclk, .bclk = bclk, .ws = ws, .dout = dout, .din = din, .invert_flags = {.mclk_inv = false, .bclk_inv = false, .ws_inv = false}}};
46: 
47:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(i2s_channel_init_std_mode(tx_handle_, &std_cfg));
48:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(i2s_channel_init_std_mode(rx_handle_, &std_cfg));
49:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Duplex channels created");
50: }
51: 
52: void Es8388AudioCodec::SetOutputVolume(int volume)
53: {
54:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_out_vol(output_dev_, volume));
55:     AudioCodec::SetOutputVolume(volume);
56: }
57: 
58: void Es8388AudioCodec::EnableInput(bool enable)
59: {
60:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(data_if_mutex_);
61:     if (enable == input_enabled_)
62:     {
63:         return;
64:     }
65:     if (enable)
66:     {
67:         esp_codec_dev_sample_info_t fs = {
68:             .bits_per_sample = 16,
69:             .channel = (uint8_t)input_channels_,
70:             .channel_mask = ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK(0),
71:             .sample_rate = (uint32_t)input_sample_rate_,
72:             .mclk_multiple = 0,
73:         };
74:         if (input_reference_)
75:         {
76:             fs.channel_mask |= ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK(1);
77:         }
78:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_open(input_dev_, &fs));
79:         if (input_reference_)
80:         {
81:             uint8_t gain = (11 << 4) + 0;
82:             ctrl_if_->write_reg(ctrl_if_, 0x09, 1, &gain, 1);
83:         }
84:         else
85:         {
86:             ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_in_gain(input_dev_, input_gain_));
87:         }
88:     }
89:     else
90:     {
91:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(input_dev_));
92:     }
93:     AudioCodec::EnableInput(enable);
94: }
95: 
96: void Es8388AudioCodec::EnableOutput(bool enable)
97: {
98:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(data_if_mutex_);
99:     if (enable == output_enabled_)
100:     {
101:         return;
102:     }
103:     if (enable)
104:     {
105:         esp_codec_dev_sample_info_t fs = {
106:             .bits_per_sample = 16,
107:             .channel = 1,
108:             .channel_mask = 0,
109:             .sample_rate = (uint32_t)output_sample_rate_,
110:             .mclk_multiple = 0,
111:         };
112:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_open(output_dev_, &fs));
113:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_out_vol(output_dev_, output_volume_));
114: 
115:         // Set analog output volume to 0dB, default is -45dB
116:         uint8_t reg_val = 30; // 0dB
117:         if (input_reference_)
118:         {
119:             reg_val = 27;
120:         }
121:         uint8_t regs[] = {46, 47, 48, 49}; // HP_LVOL, HP_RVOL, SPK_LVOL, SPK_RVOL
122:         for (uint8_t reg : regs)
123:         {
124:             ctrl_if_->write_reg(ctrl_if_, reg, 1, &reg_val, 1);
125:         }
126: 
127:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)
128:         {
129:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 1);
130:         }
131:     }
132:     else
133:     {
134:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(output_dev_));
135:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)
136:         {
137:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 0);
138:         }
139:     }
140:     AudioCodec::EnableOutput(enable);
141: }
142: 
143: int Es8388AudioCodec::Read(int16_t *dest, int samples)
144: {
145:     if (input_enabled_)
146:     {
147:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK_WITHOUT_ABORT(esp_codec_dev_read(input_dev_, (void *)dest, samples * sizeof(int16_t)));
148:     }
149:     return samples;
150: }
151: 
152: int Es8388AudioCodec::Write(const int16_t *data, int samples)
153: {
154:     if (output_enabled_ && output_dev_ && data != nullptr)
155:     {
156:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK_WITHOUT_ABORT(esp_codec_dev_write(output_dev_, (void *)data, samples * sizeof(int16_t)));
157:     }
158:     return samples;
159: }
160: 
</file>

Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to create duplex I2S channels but uses an older/deprecated I2S API (`i2s_config_t`, `i2s_driver_install`) instead of the newer channel-based API (`i2s_chan_config_t`, `i2s_new_channel`) used in the ground truth.
- It incorrectly assigns the same handle (`I2S_NUM_0`) to both `rx_handle_` and `tx_handle_`, which may not align with how these are intended to be used elsewhere.
- Sample rate logic differs slightly; it uses `input_sample_rate_` directly without asserting equality between input/output rates as in the ground truth.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly, including parameter types and order.
- Control flow diverges significantly due to different APIs being used. The ground truth separates configuration into channel creation and standard mode initialization, while the generated version bundles everything into one driver install call.
- Key structures like `i2s_std_config_t` and its nested configurations (`clk_cfg`, `slot_cfg`, `gpio_cfg`) are missing from the generated code.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality—setting up full-duplex I2S communication—is conceptually present.
- Missing features include explicit use of new I2S channel APIs, proper error checking on channel init functions, and logging confirmation ("Duplex channels created").
- Extra steps such as manual GPIO setup for MCLK suggest over-engineering or misunderstanding of the abstraction level expected by the new API.
- DMA descriptor/frame numbers, interrupt priority settings, and auto-clear behavior prior to callback are omitted.

146:         else
147:         {
148:             ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_in_gain(input_dev_, input_gain_));
149:         }
150:     }
151:     else
152:     {
153:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(input_dev_));
154:     }
155:     AudioCodec::EnableInput(enable);
156: }
157: 
158: void Es8388AudioCodec::EnableOutput(bool enable)
159: {
160:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(data_if_mutex_);
161:     if (enable == output_enabled_)
162:     {
163:         return;
164:     }
165:     if (enable)
166:     {
167:         esp_codec_dev_sample_info_t fs = {
168:             .bits_per_sample = 16,
169:             .channel = 1,
170:             .channel_mask = 0,
171:             .sample_rate = (uint32_t)output_sample_rate_,
172:             .mclk_multiple = 0,
173:         };
174:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_open(output_dev_, &fs));
175:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_out_vol(output_dev_, output_volume_));
176: 
177:         // Set analog output volume to 0dB, default is -45dB
178:         uint8_t reg_val = 30; // 0dB
179:         if (input_reference_)
180:         {
181:             reg_val = 27;
182:         }
183:         uint8_t regs[] = {46, 47, 48, 49}; // HP_LVOL, HP_RVOL, SPK_LVOL, SPK_RVOL
184:         for (uint8_t reg : regs)
185:         {
186:             ctrl_if_->write_reg(ctrl_if_, reg, 1, &reg_val, 1);
187:         }
188: 
189:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)
190:         {
191:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 1);
192:         }
193:     }
194:     else
195:     {
196:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(output_dev_));
197:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)
198:         {
199:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 0);
200:         }
201:     }
202:     AudioCodec::EnableOutput(enable);
203: }
204: 
205: int Es8388AudioCodec::Read(int16_t *dest, int samples)
206: {
207:     if (input_enabled_)
208:     {
209:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK_WITHOUT_ABORT(esp_codec_dev_read(input_dev_, (void *)dest, samples * sizeof(int16_t)));
210:     }
211:     return samples;
212: }
213: 
214: int Es8388AudioCodec::Write(const int16_t *data, int samples)
215: {
216:     if (output_enabled_ && output_dev_ && data != nullptr)
217:     {
218:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK_WITHOUT_ABORT(esp_codec_dev_write(output_dev_, (void *)data, samples * sizeof(int16_t)));
219:     }
220:     return samples;
221: }
222:

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness: The generated code attempts to implement volume setting but diverges significantly in approach. While the ground truth simply delegates to a base class and an ESP-IDF codec API, the generated version includes manual range checking, internal state storage, and direct hardware register manipulation. This suggests a misunderstanding of the abstraction layer intended by the original design. However, both codes aim to set output volume, giving partial semantic alignment.

Structure Similarity: The function signature matches, but the internal structure differs greatly. The ground truth is concise and relies on existing abstractions, whereas the generated code is verbose and assumes low-level hardware control. The use of ESP_ERROR_CHECK and esp_codec_dev_set_out_vol is shared, but the additional logic in the generated code (e.g., register writes) makes the control flow dissimilar.

Functional Completeness: The generated code does include the core ESP-IDF call (esp_codec_dev_set_out_vol), covers volume range validation, and even extends functionality by attempting direct register control. It also stores the volume internally and calls a base class method implicitly through register updates. However, it misses the explicit call to AudioCodec::SetOutputVolume(). Despite this omission, it covers most functional aspects, though with unnecessary complexity.

results.description: The `EnableInput` function controls the audio input functionality of the ES8388 codec, enabling or disabling microphone input based on the provided boolean parameter. When enabled, it configures and opens the input device with specified sample rate, bit depth, and channel settings, and applies either a fixed gain or adjustable input gain. When disabled, it closes the input device to stop audio capture.

7: Es8388AudioCodec::Es8388AudioCodec(void *i2c_master_handle, i2c_port_t i2c_port, int input_sample_rate, int output_sample_rate,
8:                                    gpio_num_t mclk, gpio_num_t bclk, gpio_num_t ws, gpio_num_t dout, gpio_num_t din,
9:                                    gpio_num_t pa_pin, uint8_t es8388_addr, bool input_reference)
10: {
11:     duplex_ = true;                             // 是否双工
12:     input_reference_ = input_reference;         // 是否使用参考输入，实现回声消除
13:     input_channels_ = input_reference_ ? 2 : 1; // 输入通道数
14:     input_sample_rate_ = input_sample_rate;
15:     output_sample_rate_ = output_sample_rate;
16:     input_gain_ = 24;
17: 
18:     pa_pin_ = pa_pin;
19:     CreateDuplexChannels(mclk, bclk, ws, dout, din);
20: 
21:     // Do initialize of related interface: data_if, ctrl_if and gpio_if
22:     audio_codec_i2s_cfg_t i2s_cfg = {
23:         .port = I2S_NUM_0,
24:         .rx_handle = rx_handle_,
25:         .tx_handle = tx_handle_,
26:     };
27:     data_if_ = audio_codec_new_i2s_data(&i2s_cfg);
28:     assert(data_if_ != NULL);
29: 
30:     // Output
31:     audio_codec_i2c_cfg_t i2c_cfg = {
32:         .port = i2c_port,
33:         .addr = es8388_addr,
34:         .bus_handle = i2c_master_handle,
35:     };
36:     ctrl_if_ = audio_codec_new_i2c_ctrl(&i2c_cfg);
37:     assert(ctrl_if_ != NULL);
38: 
39:     gpio_if_ = audio_codec_new_gpio();
40:     assert(gpio_if_ != NULL);
41:

129:     if (enable == output_enabled_)

132:     }

139:             .sample_rate = (uint32_t)output_sample_rate_,
140:             .mclk_multiple = 0,
141:         };

143:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_out_vol(output_dev_, output_volume_));
144: 
145:         // Set analog output volume to 0dB, default is -45dB
146:         uint8_t reg_val = 30; // 0dB
147:         if (input_reference_)
148:         {
149:             reg_val = 27;
150:         }
151:         uint8_t regs[] = {46, 47, 48, 49}; // HP_LVOL, HP_RVOL, SPK_LVOL, SPK_RVOL
152:         for (uint8_t reg : regs)
153:         {
154:             ctrl_if_->write_reg(ctrl_if_, reg, 1, &reg_val, 1);
155:         }
156: 
157:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)
158:         {
159:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 1);
160:         }
161:     }
162:     else
163:     {
164:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(output_dev_));
165:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)
166:         {
167:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 0);
168:         }
169:     }
170:     AudioCodec::EnableOutput(enable);
171: }
